programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. A convicted wife-killer takes Dr Sloan hostage, but Steve fears trigger-happy police officers may be the real threat to his father's life. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dr Sloan hunts for evidence that will clear the name of an innocent man before it is too late. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is deputized then called upon to transport a man charged with a crime to the county seat

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A woman whose dying husband has accused her of poisoning him asks Perry for help. When the man dies, his body goes missing, making things more complicated

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker's friend is the main suspect when an archaeologist is murdered. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio investigates when shots are fired at rap star 10-Large's concert, but after the body of a security guard is found, it seems the musician may not have been the intended target after all. While the rapper refuses to co-operate, the team enters the dangerous world of personal security. Elsewhere, Alex gets a nasty shock when the supposed victim of a car accident wakes up in the morgue. Rapper Xzibit guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his team investigate the bizarre deaths of a marine and two of his friends at a stag party. Elsewhere, Gibbs tries to persuade Vance to allow Ziva back into the NCIS. However, she must first pass a barrage of psychological evaluations. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard takes a job as lifeguard at a swanky Nevada Hotel and lands in the middle of a family feud

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry investigates when a woman is accused of her husband's murder after discovering he had been living a double life

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A navy lieutenant's car careers off the road and he is found to have been murdered behind the wheel. NCIS is called in to investigate and Tony becomes obsessed with finding the victim's sister, a well-known TV reporter whose disappearance is seemingly linked to the case - and several others. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A group of escaped convicts hide in a local church and take two nuns and Alex hostage. Guest starring, Barbara Bain

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the case of a marine who dropped dead while scaling a wall on an assault course, and a fellow soldier reveals that he was heavily involved with an underground fight club, where competitors would battle for cash. Meanwhile, having returned to work after compassionate leave, Vance discovers paperwork and a key in his late wife's effects that lead him to a safety deposit box containing a stash of cash

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a mutilated body is found in a car at a scrapyard, the NCIS agents find themselves trying to locate a serial killer. Meanwhile, Abby and McGee head to Mexico and investigate the 20-year-old mystery of a drug dealer's murder. Their actions attract the attention of the head of a local drug cartel, and links begin to emerge between their case, the serial killings and Gibbs's past

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While transporting the corpse of Lt Gordon Roth to the morgue, a flat tyre brings Ducky and Jimmy to a halt and they are abducted and taken to a remote cabin - where one of their captors wants the post-mortem to take place. Back at the scene, Abby finds a blood sample that does not belong to the victim, who in fact bore little resemblance to the picture of Roth in his old school yearbook. Guest starring Valerie Cruz (The Following)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when fellow agent Lara Macy's body is found badly burnt in a campfire on Annapolis beach. According to her laptop she was tailing an unco-operative rape victim, and the prime suspect in the crime is the son of a DC contractor. Gibbs knew Macy years ago, and he finds he can no longer avoid his Mexican past catching up with him as the case progresses. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Dean captures Gibbs in Mexico and hands him over to Paloma Reynosa, head of a drug cartel and daughter of the man he killed. Meanwhile, Ziva is accepted as a US citizen

Rural drama, starring Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. Heaton's first suspect provides an alibi for one of the cold case murders and finds himself in the clear as forensic evidence establishes that all three girls were killed in the same manner. Desperate for his freedom, he cooperates with the police by identifying everyone involved in staging the rave events attended by the victims. When the real killer is named, the race is on to find him before he strikes again. James Gaddas guest stars

programme.desc: Sahira fumes after Hanssen takes her off CTU, and finds no relief at home when Rafi quizzes her about her bond with Greg. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Guppy takes heroin by mistake after stealing what he thought was cocaine from a patient's pocket. Elyes Gabel and Jane Hazlegrove star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a cab driver is badly beaten and his takings stolen, an electronic travel card found near the scene provides a lead, linking the crime to a volunteer worker at a support centre on the Jasmine Allen estate. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Barry tells Roy that Vanessa has fleeced him. Word spreads around the Square that Mary has run off with Joe; Lorraine and Pauline give Mary a telling off

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian and Sanjay are concerned about the uncollected rubbish around the Square. Sarah is upset and confused over Joe and confides in the vicar, Alex

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: The Jersey detective becomes an unwelcome guest at a wedding while investigating an attack on a curate. Drama, starring John Nettles and Terence Alexander

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Good Samaritan Alison offers sanctuary to a mugging victim - only to discover during a murder inquiry that her guest is involved in smuggling illegal immigrants into Britain as cheap labour. Meanwhile, Angela is surprised to find she has a secret admirer. Drama, starring Tamzin Malleson and Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A stranger asks for help with a leaking car radiator shortly before police turn up at the house, but it transpires he has a hidden agenda. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The day of the annual Yellowcoat competition dawns - and the stakes are raised when a Caribbean holiday is up for grabs. Comedy, starring Su Pollard and Paul Shane

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey reprimands DeVere for not fulfilling his duties as lord of the manor when he fails to attend church on Sunday, but then forgets to practise what she preaches. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A hang-gliding enthusiast crashes down in the village, mistakenly believing that he's landed in France, thus breaking the long-distance record. The gang tries to compensate for his disappointment by making him the best at something else - identifying a strange cat creature lurking in the woods. Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Brian Murphy star

programme.title: Inspector Alleyn Mysteries
programme.desc: An investigation gets under way when barrister Luke Watchman dies of cyanide poisoning during his annual holiday at an inn. Crime drama, starring Patrick Malahide, Kevin McNally and William Simons

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The gun used to kill a boxer is discovered at the scene of an armed robbery 15 years later, prompting the team to reinvestigate the case. Twenty-one-year-old Eddie Mayfair was shot dead a week after winning an important bout, which left his opponent seriously injured and unable to carry on his career. The detectives initially suspect revenge as a motive, but the case is complicated when they uncover the rivalries and money-making aspects of the sport. Guest starring Maggie O'Neill (Shameless) and Chloe Howman (Casualty)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part crime drama. Connor and Walker fear for the children's safety as their suspicions about Harwood are confirmed, and when they finally catch up with him it seems he has lost his mind. David Hayman, Victoria Smurfit and Charles Dance star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The cold case squad investigates the grisly unsolved murder of a headmaster, when another man is found killed in very similar circumstances, and they begin to suspect the victims may have been targeted because of their sexuality. However, their efforts are complicated by the Organised Crime Unit, who are also assigned to the case and have very different ideas about why these men were killed. Phil Daniels guest stars

programme.desc: Mary and Sarah compete for Joe's affections. Huw, Lenny and Robbie are sacked; Susan goes to have tests in hospital

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sarah decides to confront Joe. Roy realises he can't keep the truth from Pat any longer, and he finally tells her Barry's wedding is off and they have to sell the business

programme.desc: On returning to Ash Park, George tells the family that he is going to make an important announcement at dinner and invites James for a private chat beforehand. Gino is reluctant to face James, knowing his secret and Sarah and Rene's first moment together since their reunion is spoilt when he has another seizure and she is haunted by memories from the war. Romantic drama, starring Brett Climo and Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Jim believes a former golf pro is behind a spate of robberies but, with no proof to back up his suspicions, he cannot prevent the suspect from leaving Jersey. Drama, starring John Nettles and Sean Arnold

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul draws closer to DS Helen Diamond, but his social life is disrupted by the discovery that a female acquaintance of close friend Brian Taylor has drowned in mysterious circumstances. Burns on the body and links with an illegal laboratory suggest this is not an open-and-shut case and inspire the doctor's resolve to get at the truth. Nigel Le Vaillant and Michael Elphick star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: An encounter with an old flame makes Sharon feel broody, while Tracey has her work cut out pampering Garth in the school holidays. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin demotes Gladys and replaces her with a new supervisor, who requires her own chalet - throwing the accommodation situation into chaos. Comedy, starring Simon Cadell and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere moves his multinational grocery business to the manor and clashes with Audrey over the removal of a fireplace that he considers ugly and she believes is intrinsic to the property. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly and Clegg are enlightened in the wisdom of the East by an electrical handyman from Hull, and Howard struggles to keep his self-styled bandit persona alive. Meanwhile, a debt-collector is an unwelcome guest for Tom

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A priest is murdered in a tiny village, the site of a hugely publicised miracle cure a few weeks earlier, and the crime-solving duo arrive to find a community deeply divided and steeped in superstition. Searching for a likely suspect, they instead discover the body of a girl killed 40 years previously. James Bolam, Lindsey Coulson and Roger Lloyd-Pack guest star, with Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Retired DI Frank Patterson presents the team with information regarding the getaway car used in a 30-year-old safety deposit-box robbery. They discover the former detective not only wants to convict the suspected thief of the heist and murder of his three colleagues, but also prove that a high-ranking officer was involved in the plot - jeopardising the future of Sandra's Ucos unit. Phil Daniels guest stars

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: First of a two-part story by Lynda La Plante in which the team investigate the death of a prostitute who plunged from the balcony of a block of luxury apartments. They discover she was the daughter of a famous Seventies TV star murdered 12 years previously, whose killer was never traced, and try to find a link between the two crimes. David Hayman stars as the newly promoted DCS Michael Walker, alongside Victoria Smurfit, Colin Salmon and Frances Barber

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A psychiatrist treating a disturbed woman on trial for killing her family asks the team to investigate possible links to a double murder committed three years before, and their inquiries soon turn up a number of similarities. Paul Kaye, Laurence Penry-Jones and Lucy Gaskell guest star, with Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Guppy's addiction to cocaine causes him to make a potentially fatal error, but a guilt-racked Stitch takes the blame. Maggie urges Selena to tell Harry how she feels about him as he tours the hospital with a government minister, and a gun-toting patient puts lives in danger

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nate investigates a knife attack on young girl Aisha, only for the case to take on a personal note when he discovers his new love interest, nurse Maria Joseph, agreed not to report the injury when she treated the wounded girl in hospital. Further evidence reveals that Aisha may be the victim of a stalker - and when she is kidnapped, the race is on to rescue her from a potential killer

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lorraine discovers that Joe has slept with Mary. Pat and Roy will have to sell their house due to the debts. Ian fires Ross the private detective

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Diana tells Mark that Thomas has asked her to go away with him and leave Jacques with Ricky and Bianca. Ricky is hopeful to move into Lorraine's flat when she leaves for Bolton

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth finds out that Sarah is also in Sydney and arranges a meeting, Olivia and Carolyn make preparations for a card party for Sir Richard and George discovers who Anna's real father is

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Two corpses are discovered in the sea, and Jim is puzzled to learn one has only been dead a few hours, while the other drowned 40 years before. John Nettles, Cecile Paoli and Terence Alexander star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul answers a routine call to treat a woman slightly injured in a road accident, but walks into the life-or-death nightmare of an armed siege. Detective drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey decides to make an effort with her in-laws for Darryl's sake - but soon lives to regret it. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Barry and Yvonne tread the light fantastic, and Spike has a humorous conversation with Pinocchio, while Ted attempts to foil Joe Maplin's expansion plans. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere neglects to invite Audrey to the annual Hunt Ball - so she plans a socially acceptable revenge. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After helping Entwistle repair his truck, Truly, Clegg and Billy find their mechanical skills are put to the test once more when they help Tom and Smiler locate the source of an irritating squeak on their handcart. However, when Barry's longed-for game of golf evaporates thanks to his borrowed car breaking down, all the trio can do is help him drown his sorrows

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Tensions rise to deadly levels when a performance troupe of anarchists arrive in Kembleford, leaving Mrs McCarthy unimpressed and Bunty inspired

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Tommy urges Frances to go further with her attempts at making Catherine's life a misery, and a therapist tries to understand why Catherine attended Tommy Lee Royce's mother's funeral. Meanwhile, John begins to sweat when a new body is discovered. Crime drama, starring Sarah Lancashire, James Norton and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The veteran detectives investigate the murder of a palaeontologist at the Natural History Museum, discovering the victim was a respected but outspoken scientist with a knack for rubbing people up the wrong way. When it comes out he was strongly opposed to the museum's sponsorship deal with a large fuel company, the cops soon have plenty of suspects. Amanda Redman stars, with guest appearances by Trevor Bannister, Lucy Brown and Natasha Little

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Concluding part. Jonathan Southwood is taken in for questioning, during which a series of shocking revelations point to the true murderer. Walker faces a race against time to reach Connor, who is working undercover at a private club where Colin Thorpe is putting her professionalism to the ultimate test. Crime drama, starring Victoria Smurfit, David Hayman and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A mummified body found on a derelict aeroplane leads Boyd and the team to a man convicted in a similar case, who Spence gets to know undercover. The victim's uncle reveals the dead man's connections with a corrupt businessman selling fake drugs - but a tragedy foils the detectives' work. Crime drama, starring Trevor Eve and Wil Johnson

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a former commercial unit in Madeley in Staffordshire, a fire-damaged house in Wolverhampton and a former care home in Ilkeston in Derbyshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the controversial killing of a police officer which sparked the round-up of dozens of suspects and ended with three men going to the gallows

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp takes a trip along Pontcysyllte aqueduct

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, featuring action from the outside courts

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer demonstrated why they were still the top two seeds after many years at the pinnacle of the game when they recorded victories over David Goffin and Kei Nishikori respectively at this stage in 2019, and the two eventually met in the final, with Djokovic prevailing

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Given the hospital's chequered history, everyone is anxious and on high alert as the Care Quality Commission conducts an emergency inspection. Josh's mother puts pressure on his age-gap romance with Ange, while doubt is cast on a patient Rich Peterson's claim to have been bitten by a dog

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes more people with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. First through the doors is Graeme with a fairground dodgem that he hopes will bump up the bids from the dealers. Gloria brings in an electric massager that she bought at a car-boot sale, and Jonathan hopes to sell a piece of mysterious motoring memorabilia, which even has auctioneer Simon Bower perplexed

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Toy expert David Burville and the 'teddy bear ladies' Julie and Amanda team up to restore a long-retired Victorian curiosity, a piano-playing doll powered by clockwork. Steve Fletcher needs his mechanical expertise as he works on a vintage bus conductor's ticket machine, Kirsten Ramsay tries to repair a fire-damaged trophy belonging to a former world champion kickboxer and the team are faced with an unusual reminder of a secret agent's wartime record

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are joined by former England star Alex Scott and comedian Jack Whitehall for the last of their live shows, as they react to the concluding semi-final and look forward to the final

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: England v Denmark. Gary Lineker presents action from the second semi-final, as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals met at Wembley Stadium. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium, as the line-up for Sunday's showpiece match was confirmed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit properties in Gillingham in Kent, Chaddesden in Derby and Burslem in Stoke-on-Trent, all of which have different renovation issues to resolve before they can be sold

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1863 in which engine tender James Barton was murdered and his prized silver pocket watch stolen. The family of 30-year-old blacksmith Thomas Grime handed in the pocket watch and when questioned Grime admitted being present when the murder was committed, but accused others of the fatal assault. However, Grime alone was convicted of murder

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain in Shetland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Gary Pe help two teams hunt for items at the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, while Eric Knowles explores the Japanese concept of wabi-sabi in pottery

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 10 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the ladies' singles semi-finals are scheduled take place, featuring Aryna Sabalenka v Karolina Pliskova and Ashleigh Barty v Angelique Kerber. Kerber is the only one of these players to have previously won this title, having defeated Serena Williams in the 2018 final, but any of the four would make a worthy champion given the fine form they have all displayed so far in this year's tournament

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Ronan Keating and Clara Amfo host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is angry when Denise refuses to let him see Raymond again, but refuses to give up without a fight. Lola realises she needs to step up as Isaac returns home, especially as Sheree thinks all her son needs is a few weeks' rest

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies encounter a digital entrepreneur passionate about the potential of her online hypno-birthing platforms and two north-east business partners with a unique recycled building system. A dynamic pair of jewellers charm the potential investors with their bespoke ring-making kits, while another hopeful anticipates the Dragons will get stuck into his plant-based, biodegradable chewing gum. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Young offender Michaella McCollum, who became infamous worldwide as one half of the Peru Two, offers her account of how she came to be arrested, tried and jailed in one of the most notorious maximum security prisons in Peru. She recounts how she and fellow would-be mule Melissa Reid were tasked with pretending to be tourists at Machu Picchu, before walking 12kg of cocaine into Jorge Chávez International Airport in August 2013

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Micah Richards to count down the best teams in the history of the European Championship

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-time investor takes on a garden level flat in London's Crystal Palace, Dion Dublin meets a lorry driver who's renovating a mid-terrace house in Stoke-on Trent, and in Strood, Kent, a Navy reservist transforms a three-storey townhouse into a stylish holiday let

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1882, when two boys making their way home from school saw two men in distinctive long black coats shoot Co Kerry farmer and landowner Thomas Browne dead. Despite no murder weapon being recovered, unemployed tenant farmer Sylvester Poff and his cousin James Barrett were arrested, charged and later found guilty of Browne's murder

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dr Lalla is frustrated by summer fog, which means the air ambulance cannot land. To get his patient to specialist care, he urgently needs the coastguard helicopter, and the fog to clear. A South African with Shetland rootsis persuaded to get his foot X-rayed after a nasty stumble, and a fracture is diagnosed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes a look at the rarest and most unique items bought by past contestants and also finds out about a rare book on birds worth millions of pounds. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp talks to Charles Hanson about Faberge flowers, and Charlie Ross explores a unique collection of self-playing instruments

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 11 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles semi-finals are scheduled take place. Novak Djokovic booked his place in the final at this stage two years ago with a 6-2, 4-6, 6-3, 6-2 victory over Roberto Bautista Agut. His opponent was once again confirmed as Roger Federer, after he overcame old rival Rafael Nadal 7-6, 1-6, 6-3, 6-4

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Jay Blades, Liz Bonnin, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Greg James, Sue Johnston, Guz Khan, Alice Levine, Steph McGovern, Stephen Merchant, Bob Mortimer, Sharon Osbourne, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders, Ranj Singh, Joe Sugg, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack and Sally Bretton return for the domestic comedy, which sees the couple continuing to go from one disaster to the next, while coping with their three kids, Lucy's disapproving parents, Lee's feckless dad Frank and world-weary friends Toby and Anna. In the first episode, Geoffrey reluctantly allows Lee to help Lucy clear up the builder's mess in their new kitchen extension. With Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's plans hit a brick wall when solicitor Ritchie is too busy to take on the custody case, so Ben offers to help. Frankie is nervous when the police arrive with a lead on Nancy's case, Chelsea tries to persuade Gray to let her move in - in her own seductive way - and Bobby is flustered when Dana is arrives at the restaurant. The Walford regulars hear a rumour that football legend Harry Redknapp is making an appearance at the Vic for the Euros. Word is, he's a friend of Rocky - but no one believes a word he says

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Out of Sight (1998)
programme.desc: A charming bank robber breaks out of jail and takes a female US Marshal hostage. Managing to escape, she sets out to track him down as he prepares for one last job - but as the chase intensifies, so do their feelings for each other. Steven Soderbergh's romantic crime drama, based on Elmore Leonard's novel, with George Clooney, Jennifer Lopez, Ving Rhames and Don Cheadle

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a notorious case of supposed matricide dating from the 1920s, which may have resulted in an innocent man being hanged

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Ross presents the show from Brackley, Northamptonshire, and discovers how old food can be collectable. With experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Vilma Jackson celebrates black deaf people who have achieved extraordinary things in the UK. Plus, meeting the creator of Flow TV. With voiceover

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Wigan Warriors and Warrington Wolves v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played, as well as the ladies' doubles quarter-finals, and mixed doubles matches. Spaniards Roberto Bautista Agut and Rafael Nadal both booked semi-final spots at this stage two years ago on No. 1 Court, defeating Argentina's Guido Pella and American Sam Querrey respectively. Meanwhile, in the mixed doubles competition, top seeds Bruno Soares and Nicole Melica were able to overcome the star power of Andy Murray and Serena Williams, prevailing 6-4, 4-6, 6-2, and second ladies' doubles seeds Barbora Krejcikova and Katerina Siniakova progressed in straight sets against Anna-Lena Gronefeld and Demi Schuurs

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of day nine's play at the All England Club, where the line-up for the men's singles semi-finals was due to be decided. In addition to detailed analysis of those contests, there is a round-up of the latest headlines, as well as a look ahead to the ladies' singles semi-finals, which are scheduled for Centre Court tomorrow

programme.title: Hampton Court Palace Garden Festival
programme.desc: Joe Swift, Rachel de Thame, Adam Frost and Arit Anderson head up the coverage as the world's largest annual flower show reopens for 2021. There is a range of diverse garden designs to explore, including a lifestyle category, global impact gardens addressing environmental challenges and large show-stopping gardens and features. Also, for the first time, the presenters pair up to create a themed Hampton flower border their very own competition. With analysis and advice from Carol Klein, Toby Buckland, Nick Bailey and Mark Lane

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Josh Widdicombe, Susan Calman and Rich Hall join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Origins and Openings'. During the course of the show, the panellists find out an assortment of facts, including what Mr First thought of Mr Second. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: A Dangerous Dynasty: House of Assad
programme.desc: As a glamorous profile of Asma appears in Vogue magazine, the Arab Spring protests reach Syria. Bashar must decide whether to concede to their demands for greater freedom or follow in his father Hafez's famously hardline steps. What unfolds shocks the world in its brutality, but as the chaos of civil war rages, the so-called Islamic State emerges and it looks like Bashar is on the brink of losing the Assad dynasty's control of Syria

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Vilma Jackson celebrates black deaf people who have achieved extraordinary things in the UK. Plus, meeting the creator of Flow TV. With voiceover

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Part one of two. Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This series follows the engineers, technicians, and the behind-the-scenes staff who are under pressure to keep the project on track, including building the mammoth foundations for the two nuclear reactors, and excavating cooling water tunnels out under the Bristol Channel

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: As news of the Covid-19 virus broke around the world, teams of scientists jumped into action to create a vaccine against a virus that no one had heard of before. This programme looks at the extraordinary inside story of the unprecedented quest to develop vaccines to fight Covid-19, with unique access to five teams of scientists around the globe. Narrated by Lesley Sharp

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray take a look at the progress of the beds of wildflower seeds and turf that they sowed and laid eight weeks ago. Kirsty Wilson is also at Beechgrove and she and Carole and Mairi enjoy a masterclass on the Japanese art of Kokedama, or creating Japanese moss balls, with expert Fiona McDonald from Aberdeen

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate an infamous Scottish murder case from 1862 where a compelling alternative suspect could have been the true killer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and John Cameron help the red and blue teams search for valuable items in Leominster, Herefordshire, in the hope of making a profit at auction. Plus, host Anita Manning finds out more about Worcester's saucy secret

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An inventor looks for investment in his quirky pet products and a pair of optometrists hope the Dragons will see a bright future in their anti-blue light filtering idea. A schoolteacher-come-food entrepreneur tempts the Dragons' taste buds as well as their pockets with her low-sugar children's jams and an eco-entrepreneur pitches a carbon-offsetting trading app. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 10 of the tournament at the All England Club, including the early stages of the opening ladies' singles semi-final, with the men's doubles semi-finals, and matches in the mixed doubles also set to take place. Colombia's Juan Sebastian Cabal and French pairing Nicola Mahut and Edouard Roger-Vasselin confirmed their places in the men's doubles final two years ago after victories over Raven Klaasen and Michael Venus, and Ivan Dodig and Filip Polasek respectively. However, Dodig had the consolation of a place in the mixed doubles semi-final, after the Croatian and Chinese Taipei's Latisha Chan defeated British wildcards Evan Hoyt and Eden Silva in straight sets

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of the day's play at the All England Club, where the ladies' singles last-four matches would decide who progressed to Saturday's final. Plus, there is a round-up of the day's headlines, and a preview of the men's singles semi-finals, which are scheduled to feature on Centre Court tomorrow

programme.title: Hampton Court Palace Garden Festival
programme.desc: Joe Swift, Rachel de Thame, Adam Frost and Arit Anderson present further coverage from the grounds of Hampton Court Palace. New to the show this year are the RHS Allotments, in which community groups exhibit for the first time, and the RHS Flower Market, bringing together florists and cut-flower specialists to showcase the latest trends in displays

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A further selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. This time around, Rob Brydon, Sara Pascoe, Dara O Briain, Lou Sanders, Rich Hall, Dane Baptiste and others offer their thoughts on sex

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the first match in the three-match series, which took place at Sophia Gardens, Cardiff

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Sheriffs visit a cleaning company that owes money to a former employee, and come to the aid of a woman who took legal action against an estate agent over cleaning expenses

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works as a roadside mechanic, finding out what it takes to keep the UK's motorists moving. He follows breakdowns from the initial alert, learns techniques for roadside assistance and rescues stranded motorists with one of the largest automotive service companies in the country

programme.title: A Pandemic Poem: Where Did the World Go?
programme.desc: An examination of the pandemic, using a poem written by Poet Laureate Simon Armitage that charts the progression of Covid-19 from the early rumours arriving from Wuhan to the rollout of the vaccination programme and the easing of lockdown

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the controversial killing of a police officer which sparked the round-up of dozens of suspects and ended with three men going to the gallows

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp takes a trip along Pontcysyllte aqueduct

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Part two of two. Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This time, the team creates an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. Elsewhere, cameras follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded Second World War bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 11 of the tournament at the All England Club, including the early stages of the opening men's singles semi-final, with the last-four matches in the ladies' doubles and mixed doubles and the quad wheelchair doubles final also set to take place. The top two seeds both fell in the ladies' doubles at this stage two years, with Barbora Krejcikova and Katerina Siniakova losing out to Gabriela Dabrowski and Xu Yifan, and Timea Babos and Kristina Mladenovic suffering defeat at the hands of Hsie Su-wei and Barbora Strycova, In the mixed doubles, Robert Lindstedt and Jelena Ostapenko prevailed against Matwe Middelkoop and Yang Zhaoxuan, and Ivan Dodig and Latisha Chan joined them in the final after a victory over Wesley Koolhof and Kveta Peschke

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of the day's play, where the men's singles semi-finals were scheduled to take centre-stage at the All England Club, with the players hoping to stay in contention to succeed 2019 champion Novak Djokovic. Plus, there is a round-up of the day's headlines, and a preview of tomorrow's ladies' singles final

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don catches up with all his summer jobs in the border and the vegetable garden. Carol Klein gets inspiration for planting combinations at Cothay Manor and Gardens, and Arit Anderson learns about slugs and snails

programme.title: The Moments That Made...
programme.desc: Celebrating the life and career of actor Richard Wilson, featuring an interview with the One Foot in the Grave star and other key people who have been part of his career, including David Tennant and Arabella Weir. He talks about his humble beginnings as a lab technician from Greenock, how he went on to become one of the most recognisable faces in Britain, and why he nearly turned down the role of a lifetime

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Responding to a call of a brutal attack in a residential area of Dudley, West Midlands Police arrive on the scene to find the body of a young man. Soon afterwards, a second man, brought to hospital from the crime scene, is declared dead. Forensic Investigators must now piece together the evidence in this complex murder inquiry

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: The return of the crime drama set in the borderlands between Mexico and America. The Mayans and their Californian town suffer as federal forces crack down on all illegal activity, and grieving Galindo lets his legitimate project dry up too

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: Against better judgement, EZ puts a proposal to Bishop, rebel Adelita is handed to the Mexican authorities, and Coco goes gunning for opioids

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Report on British businesses dealing with post-Brexit trading rules six months after the UK left the EU single market, trying to maintain relationships with their European customers while also seeking new markets further afield. The programme features personal accounts from fishermen in Scotland to entrepreneurs in the home counties

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist looks at attempts to answer one of the most profound questions we can ask - are we alone in the universe? With scientists sending space probes to the furthest reaches of our solar system and beyond, the scientific search for alien life has begun and Brian explains why this quest deserves to be taken seriously, and explores the chances of extraterrestrials being found

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring the vibrant golden red glow of a campfire, complete with a cowboy resting by the crackling flames

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane treats herself to a makeover at the ship's hairdresser, while Dale and Mary enjoy a romantic rafting trip in Jamaica. The new on-board show gets ready to open - if Michelle's weakened voice can take the strain

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: Michelle Dockery narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the east Asian country, beginning on the central island of Honshu. It is home to more than 100 million people, but most of it is mountainous and this wilderness is home to an astonishing range of wildlife, and people and nature are drawn together in the most unexpected ways

programme.title: The Joy of Winning
programme.desc: A witty, mind-expanding guide in which Hannah Fry unlocks the essential strategies a person needs to get what they want - to win more of the time. The mathematician also hails 20th-century scientists John von Neumann and John Nash, who worked out the science of success and reveals how the same rules apply to the world's biggest problems - whether its avoiding nuclear annihilation or tackling climate change

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Three parents of those responsible for US high-school shootings share their deeply personal stories. They include Sue Klebold, whose son Dylan was one of the two teenagers behind the Columbine High School massacre in 1999, one of the deadliest school shootings in history

programme.title: Handmade in Japan
programme.desc: A look at the work of the Hamadas, one of Japan's most famous families of potters, following Tomoo as he creates and fires pots in a wood-fuelled kiln

programme.title: Beck
programme.desc: Martin Beck and the team investigate a drugs gang after the death of a young man, putting them on a collision course with an undercover operation. In Swedish

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring the vibrant golden red glow of a campfire, complete with a cowboy resting by the crackling flames

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane treats herself to a makeover at the ship's hairdresser, while Dale and Mary enjoy a romantic rafting trip in Jamaica. The new on-board show gets ready to open - if Michelle's weakened voice can take the strain

programme.title: The Joy of Winning
programme.desc: A witty, mind-expanding guide in which Hannah Fry unlocks the essential strategies a person needs to get what they want - to win more of the time. The mathematician also hails 20th-century scientists John von Neumann and John Nash, who worked out the science of success and reveals how the same rules apply to the world's biggest problems - whether its avoiding nuclear annihilation or tackling climate change

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an autumnal scene, using natural shades to depict a worn-down farm building surrounded by golden grassy bushes and trees

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: While Sean tries to impress 300 born-again Christians with his juggling skills, Jane's confidence is knocked for six when half the audience walk out during her act. In the galley, preparations begin to serve up one of the most extravagant buffets ever seen on the high seas

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: Rupert Penry-Jones stars in this adaptation of John Buchan's classic novel, set in 1914. Engineer Richard Hannay is bored and restless in London - until he meets Scudder, who claims to be a British spy. When the agent is killed in his flat, Hannay is thrown headlong into adventure, forced to go on the run under suspicion of murder. Fleeing to Scotland, with police and spies on his tail, he teams up with feisty suffragette Victoria Sinclair, and although they fail to hit it off, the couple become increasingly reliant on each other as they race to unmask a traitor and save Great Britain from German invasion. Action thriller, co-starring Eddie Marsan and Lydia Leonard

programme.title: John Buchan: Master of Suspense
programme.desc: Profile of author John Buchan, who is most famous for writing the iconic novel The Thirty Nine Steps, and is regarded as one of the most unfairly overlooked figures in British literature. Using his published works, private correspondence and papers, the documentary attempts to finally afford the writer the attention many feel he deserves. Featuring contributions from Ken Follett, Alexander Armstrong and Douglas Hurd

programme.title: Clash of the Titans (1981)
programme.desc: A hero of ancient Greece vows to save a princess from being sacrificed to a ferocious sea monster. However, to defeat the creature he must first embark on a journey into the underworld and seize the head of the monstrous Medusa, whose gaze turns men to stone. Mythical adventure, starring Harry Hamlin, Laurence Olivier, Maggie Smith and Judi Bowker, with special effects by Ray Harryhausen

programme.title: Hemingway
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of Ernest Hemingway, considered one of the greatest American writers. This instalment revisits the publication of Our Time and The Sun Also Rises, and the later critical and commercial success he enjoyed with his second novel, A Farewell to Arms. Narrated by Peter Coyote

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Formerly incarcerated activist LaTonya Myers begins work at the public defenders' office, while dealing with a stressful new probation sentence of nearly a decade. Going into his second year, Krasner makes reforming probation a major goal, but he must win over the judges to make this happen

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: While Sean tries to impress 300 born-again Christians with his juggling skills, Jane's confidence is knocked for six when half the audience walk out during her act. In the galley, preparations begin to serve up one of the most extravagant buffets ever seen on the high seas

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: Rupert Penry-Jones stars in this adaptation of John Buchan's classic novel, set in 1914. Engineer Richard Hannay is bored and restless in London - until he meets Scudder, who claims to be a British spy. When the agent is killed in his flat, Hannay is thrown headlong into adventure, forced to go on the run under suspicion of murder. Fleeing to Scotland, with police and spies on his tail, he teams up with feisty suffragette Victoria Sinclair, and although they fail to hit it off, the couple become increasingly reliant on each other as they race to unmask a traitor and save Great Britain from German invasion. Action thriller, co-starring Eddie Marsan and Lydia Leonard

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: The Herculis EBS. Coverage of the seventh meeting of the campaign, held at Stade Louis II, as the athletes fine-tune preparations for the Olympics. Uganda's Joshua Cheptegei broke the world 5,000m record here last year, and world-lead times were set in a number of other events, including the men's 200m and the women's 5,000m

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner presents the edition first aired on April 11 1991, featuring performances by the Wonderstuff, Alison Limerick, Gary Clail On-U Sound System, Mike & the Mechanics, Dannii Minogue, Madonna, Monie Love versus Adeva, Chesney Hawkes and the Shamen

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles presents the edition first aired on April 18 1991, featuring performances by James, Orchestral Manoeuvres in the Dark, Black Box, the Mock Turtles, Zucchero & Paul Young, Cher, the Clash, Chesney Hawkes and the Simpsons

programme.title: The Beatles: Made on Merseyside
programme.desc: Few dispute that the Beatles defined 1960s music and popular culture like no other band, but how John, Paul, George and Ringo made the journey from Merseyside teenagers to international pop stars is less known. Director Alan Byron's documentary recounts the rise of the band as American rock 'n' roll and rhythm and blues dragged post-war Liverpool into one of the most vibrant music cities ever with the Mersey Beat sound

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyle's comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: Sings the Beatles
programme.desc: A look back through the BBC's archives at memorable covers of classic Beatles songs. Included are a performance of Day Tripper by Sandie Shaw, Shirley Bassey's version of Something, the Carpenters' rendition of Help!, Oasis's reinvention of I Am the Walrus, and Joe Cocker's chart-topping interpretation of With a Little Help from My Friends. Plus, Paul McCartney's solo performance of Lady Madonna

programme.desc: Set 20 years in the future, Endlings is the empowering story of four teenagers in foster care who discover they're not alone in the universe, even though sometimes it can feel like they are. Halfway across the world, Tuko is alone. After the death of her mate, Tuko becomes the last elephant in existence--what biologists call an endling. Her endling status summons to Earth a mysterious Alien who is on an intergalactic quest to save the last member of near-extinct species. After Tuko is collected, the Alien's ship malfunctions and sends it on a crash course with the farm where Julia (Kamaia Fairburn), Johnny (Edison Grant), Tabby (Michela Luci) and Finn (Cale Thomas Ferrin) live with their foster dad, Mr. Leopold (Neil Crone). programme.desc: Quirky animation for pre-schoolers based on the books by Rachel Bright. A hairy hero searches for the right thing to do in a world in which he is one of a kind
