programme.desc: The letters try to sing the alphabet song, and although it takes a few tries for them to get it right, they have a lot of fun

programme.title: Kit and Pup
programme.desc: The duo play with a cardboard box

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of trees to see how we can help look after them and our planet. Discover how trees grow, how we can tell the age of trees and how the roots, leaves and bark work. Maddie shows how trees are home to lots of other plants, animals, birds and insects who live on and amongst them and have an important role in our ecosystem. We’ll discover a tree’s leaves make energy using sunlight, so they can keep growing. We learn that a tree is alive, it drinks and breathes and grows just like we do! And we’ll find out about deciduous trees that lose their leaves in winter and evergreens that keep their leaves all year round.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo can make Tee crack-up laughing with one tickle, but Tee is unable to get her to laugh in return. Tee gets more and more frustrated as he tries to find Mo's tickle spot

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The gang celebrates the four seasons, and sing a folksy song about all the exciting things each new time of the year brings

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say book and we go on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog goes back to read the very first book he read - a simple picture book.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny tries to help a butterfly that has flown inside the nursery and landed on Sula's painting

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: Great BigHoo, Toodloo, Chickedy and Chick stroke a woman's soft, fluffy hat as they learn more about soft things. Narrated by Linda Bassett

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin meets a monkey-loving friend. Mr Tumble plays hide and seek with the toys

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The friends ride the Dup Dup and the Tubby Custard Machine also takes them for a jaunt. In Tummy Tales, a boy goes on a cable car

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with eggbox and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When the Squirrels find Duggee playing saxophone, they want to have a go too

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: The monsters create fake news when they make a school newspaper.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: B.O.T. tries to keep up with a family of Huggy Rollers as they roll along together. B.O.T. ends up encased in a snowball, but the snow melts away when the Rollers give it a big warm hug!

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day tries to keep up with young animals 'on the move'. The sengi is one of the fastest small mammals on Earth, while in the Arctic guillemot chicks leap from clifftop nests. Howeverm chacma baboons walk upright to reach the best food

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to the place they can do their recycling. On the way they climb a lookout tower, listen to the wind blowing in the trees, play musical statues and even beep like reversing buses!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: See a food mosaic of a balloon and a pilot made of beads, and watch Book Girl milk a cow.

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Rebecca steps through the magic curtain and becomes a clown. She arrives at a circus where fellow entertainer Wizzo is ill and finds herself asked to step in to save the show

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Norbet creates a newspaper, but nobody's news is exciting enough. The Olobobs make Ed who reminds them they can all make exciting news with just a few sheets of paper

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grandmaster Glitch gets his hands on a GO Force, the forecast isn't good for the Tarbela Dam in Pakistan

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: To complete Mrs Pickles birthday surprise, Andy Day travels back in time 36 million years to find a tooth belonging to a giant carnivorous predator. However, when his trusty backpack is stolen, he enlists the help of a herd of giant brontothere to get it back. With Elaine Claxton

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Doug, Jess and Gramps take Waffle to his first puppy training class

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Jack and Salty help a seahorse named Sam race Speedy, who is the fastest fish in the sea

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Dad upsets Tig by dismantling the old cot in her room, but Woolly shows her that changes can be fun

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'boots' prior to going on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The friends have fun with letters A and T, and learn the words mat, cat, rat, bat, hat and vat

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five gets ready to perform at the Tower of Rock with a little help from the Five Times Table. Learn the five times table with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug find Granny Pug by a pyramid

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: In the real world Messy is bored of his dino-jigsaw! In Okido, his friends help him piece together a real prehistoric puzzle

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour yellow, from cheese to chicks, and we explore yellow in spring time. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour yellow.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin Fletcher and his friends go on an adventure to a dinosaur park. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble visits Shopkeeper Tumble in The Shop with the Lot to buy Tumble Ted a birthday present

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The friends take selfies with the Tubby Phone and a child in Tummy Tales has their photo taken in a photo booth with their grandparent

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The playful rabbit wants to draw a big picture, so Flop finds him some chalk to create a stripy, spotty, zig-zag dinosaur on the pavement

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When the Grimbots add Glitch's face to those of the presidents of Mount Rushmore, they cause a major mudslide

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: A new go-kart appears in the playground and all the youngsters want to have a go on it

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: Agility. Today at Pupton Park, Rozzie learns a lesson from Geraldine at her 'Super Fast Circus Class'. Meanwhile Rachel Yankey twists and turns with her team as they learn about agility

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace drives a futuristic solar racecar, shows off an electric ferry and goes fishing for rubbish in a boat made out of recycled plastic bottles

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain is surprised to find Cook and Line on their best behaviour, after they become tired of losing games, and decide to be good, like the Swashbucklers

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When Egg, the cheeky little chick goes on the run again, Duggee whistles to get him back

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter, Lily and Benjamin accidentally spill Mittens' milk, they launch a daring mission to replace it

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The vegetables contemplate what it would be like to live on a farm

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate sees how a refrigerator works and visits a farm where mushrooms are grown all year round

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: It’s lunchtime on Bluey's back deck! But every BBQ needs a side salad, so Bingo’s relaxing sit-down will have to wait until she finds every colour of pepper her cousins want!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are tasked with retrieving Xuli's treasured Academy Ace flying trophy from Caernarfon Castle in Wales

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly examines her shelf of special things at the back of Mack’s Stall. She chooses toy alphabet bricks that spell the word ‘yes’ and remembers the day she got them. Alice is playing ‘The Yes Game’. If you say ‘yes’ you are out, and the children enjoy joining in. The kids decide they want to beat the grown-ups at the game, and soon everyone at the Big Hub is playing. Some grown-ups are better at it than others, and Moira finds herself helping Mrs Juniper with all sorts of odd jobs around the Wobbly Coffee Pot Café in her quest to win the game – much to Mrs Juniper’s amusement. Meanwhile, Molly’s mind is on other things, as she wonders what Alice would say if Dad asked her to marry him. Soon, as ‘The Yes Game’ spreads, she thinks of a very clever way to find out.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran go to the museum to find a giant fossilised Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: John's yellow ball is too bouncy, but Sarah think she knows where the solution can be found

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: In the Toy House kitchen, Mr Onion and Pepi Nana find the little toy tub

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The friends go for a ride in the Ninky Nonk, leaving Makka Pakka behind

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Join Luke Evans for a Bedtime Story about two best friends, Bear and Duck

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a former commercial unit in Madeley in Staffordshire, a fire-damaged house in Wolverhampton and a former care home in Ilkeston in Derbyshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the controversial killing of a police officer which sparked the round-up of dozens of suspects and ended with three men going to the gallows

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp takes a trip along Pontcysyllte aqueduct

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, featuring action from the outside courts

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer demonstrated why they were still the top two seeds after many years at the pinnacle of the game when they recorded victories over David Goffin and Kei Nishikori respectively at this stage in 2019, and the two eventually met in the final, with Djokovic prevailing

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Given the hospital's chequered history, everyone is anxious and on high alert as the Care Quality Commission conducts an emergency inspection. Josh's mother puts pressure on his age-gap romance with Ange, while doubt is cast on a patient Rich Peterson's claim to have been bitten by a dog

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes more people with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. First through the doors is Graeme with a fairground dodgem that he hopes will bump up the bids from the dealers. Gloria brings in an electric massager that she bought at a car-boot sale, and Jonathan hopes to sell a piece of mysterious motoring memorabilia, which even has auctioneer Simon Bower perplexed

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Toy expert David Burville and the 'teddy bear ladies' Julie and Amanda team up to restore a long-retired Victorian curiosity, a piano-playing doll powered by clockwork. Steve Fletcher needs his mechanical expertise as he works on a vintage bus conductor's ticket machine, Kirsten Ramsay tries to repair a fire-damaged trophy belonging to a former world champion kickboxer and the team are faced with an unusual reminder of a secret agent's wartime record

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are joined by former England star Alex Scott and comedian Jack Whitehall for the last of their live shows, as they react to the concluding semi-final and look forward to the final

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: England v Denmark. Gary Lineker presents action from the second semi-final, as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals met at Wembley Stadium. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium, as the line-up for Sunday's showpiece match was confirmed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit properties in Gillingham in Kent, Chaddesden in Derby and Burslem in Stoke-on-Trent, all of which have different renovation issues to resolve before they can be sold

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1863 in which engine tender James Barton was murdered and his prized silver pocket watch stolen. The family of 30-year-old blacksmith Thomas Grime handed in the pocket watch and when questioned Grime admitted being present when the murder was committed, but accused others of the fatal assault. However, Grime alone was convicted of murder

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain in Shetland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Gary Pe help two teams hunt for items at the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, while Eric Knowles explores the Japanese concept of wabi-sabi in pottery

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 10 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the ladies' singles semi-finals are scheduled take place, featuring Aryna Sabalenka v Karolina Pliskova and Ashleigh Barty v Angelique Kerber. Kerber is the only one of these players to have previously won this title, having defeated Serena Williams in the 2018 final, but any of the four would make a worthy champion given the fine form they have all displayed so far in this year's tournament

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Ronan Keating and Clara Amfo host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is angry when Denise refuses to let him see Raymond again, but refuses to give up without a fight. Lola realises she needs to step up as Isaac returns home, especially as Sheree thinks all her son needs is a few weeks' rest

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies encounter a digital entrepreneur passionate about the potential of her online hypno-birthing platforms and two north-east business partners with a unique recycled building system. A dynamic pair of jewellers charm the potential investors with their bespoke ring-making kits, while another hopeful anticipates the Dragons will get stuck into his plant-based, biodegradable chewing gum. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Young offender Michaella McCollum, who became infamous worldwide as one half of the Peru Two, offers her account of how she came to be arrested, tried and jailed in one of the most notorious maximum security prisons in Peru. She recounts how she and fellow would-be mule Melissa Reid were tasked with pretending to be tourists at Machu Picchu, before walking 12kg of cocaine into Jorge Chávez International Airport in August 2013

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Micah Richards to count down the best teams in the history of the European Championship

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-time investor takes on a garden level flat in London's Crystal Palace, Dion Dublin meets a lorry driver who's renovating a mid-terrace house in Stoke-on Trent, and in Strood, Kent, a Navy reservist transforms a three-storey townhouse into a stylish holiday let

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1882, when two boys making their way home from school saw two men in distinctive long black coats shoot Co Kerry farmer and landowner Thomas Browne dead. Despite no murder weapon being recovered, unemployed tenant farmer Sylvester Poff and his cousin James Barrett were arrested, charged and later found guilty of Browne's murder

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dr Lalla is frustrated by summer fog, which means the air ambulance cannot land. To get his patient to specialist care, he urgently needs the coastguard helicopter, and the fog to clear. A South African with Shetland rootsis persuaded to get his foot X-rayed after a nasty stumble, and a fracture is diagnosed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes a look at the rarest and most unique items bought by past contestants and also finds out about a rare book on birds worth millions of pounds. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp talks to Charles Hanson about Faberge flowers, and Charlie Ross explores a unique collection of self-playing instruments

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 11 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles semi-finals are scheduled take place. Novak Djokovic booked his place in the final at this stage two years ago with a 6-2, 4-6, 6-3, 6-2 victory over Roberto Bautista Agut. His opponent was once again confirmed as Roger Federer, after he overcame old rival Rafael Nadal 7-6, 1-6, 6-3, 6-4

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Jay Blades, Liz Bonnin, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Greg James, Sue Johnston, Guz Khan, Alice Levine, Steph McGovern, Stephen Merchant, Bob Mortimer, Sharon Osbourne, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders, Ranj Singh, Joe Sugg, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack and Sally Bretton return for the domestic comedy, which sees the couple continuing to go from one disaster to the next, while coping with their three kids, Lucy's disapproving parents, Lee's feckless dad Frank and world-weary friends Toby and Anna. In the first episode, Geoffrey reluctantly allows Lee to help Lucy clear up the builder's mess in their new kitchen extension. With Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's plans hit a brick wall when solicitor Ritchie is too busy to take on the custody case, so Ben offers to help. Frankie is nervous when the police arrive with a lead on Nancy's case, Chelsea tries to persuade Gray to let her move in - in her own seductive way - and Bobby is flustered when Dana is arrives at the restaurant. The Walford regulars hear a rumour that football legend Harry Redknapp is making an appearance at the Vic for the Euros. Word is, he's a friend of Rocky - but no one believes a word he says

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.title: Out of Sight (1998)
programme.desc: A charming bank robber breaks out of jail and takes a female US Marshal hostage. Managing to escape, she sets out to track him down as he prepares for one last job - but as the chase intensifies, so do their feelings for each other. Steven Soderbergh's romantic crime drama, based on Elmore Leonard's novel, with George Clooney, Jennifer Lopez, Ving Rhames and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies encounter a digital entrepreneur passionate about the potential of her online hypno-birthing platforms and two north-east business partners with a unique recycled building system. A dynamic pair of jewellers charm the potential investors with their bespoke ring-making kits, while another hopeful anticipates the Dragons will get stuck into his plant-based, biodegradable chewing gum. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Young offender Michaella McCollum, who became infamous worldwide as one half of the Peru Two, offers her account of how she came to be arrested, tried and jailed in one of the most notorious maximum security prisons in Peru. She recounts how she and fellow would-be mule Melissa Reid were tasked with pretending to be tourists at Machu Picchu, before walking 12kg of cocaine into Jorge Chávez International Airport in August 2013

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Micah Richards to count down the best teams in the history of the European Championship

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-time investor takes on a garden level flat in London's Crystal Palace, Dion Dublin meets a lorry driver who's renovating a mid-terrace house in Stoke-on Trent, and in Strood, Kent, a Navy reservist transforms a three-storey townhouse into a stylish holiday let

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1882, when two boys making their way home from school saw two men in distinctive long black coats shoot Co Kerry farmer and landowner Thomas Browne dead. Despite no murder weapon being recovered, unemployed tenant farmer Sylvester Poff and his cousin James Barrett were arrested, charged and later found guilty of Browne's murder

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dr Lalla is frustrated by summer fog, which means the air ambulance cannot land. To get his patient to specialist care, he urgently needs the coastguard helicopter, and the fog to clear. A South African with Shetland rootsis persuaded to get his foot X-rayed after a nasty stumble, and a fracture is diagnosed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes a look at the rarest and most unique items bought by past contestants and also finds out about a rare book on birds worth millions of pounds. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp talks to Charles Hanson about Faberge flowers, and Charlie Ross explores a unique collection of self-playing instruments

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 11 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles semi-finals are scheduled take place. Novak Djokovic booked his place in the final at this stage two years ago with a 6-2, 4-6, 6-3, 6-2 victory over Roberto Bautista Agut. His opponent was once again confirmed as Roger Federer, after he overcame old rival Rafael Nadal 7-6, 1-6, 6-3, 6-4

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Jay Blades, Liz Bonnin, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Greg James, Sue Johnston, Guz Khan, Alice Levine, Steph McGovern, Stephen Merchant, Bob Mortimer, Sharon Osbourne, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders, Ranj Singh, Joe Sugg, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack and Sally Bretton return for the domestic comedy, which sees the couple continuing to go from one disaster to the next, while coping with their three kids, Lucy's disapproving parents, Lee's feckless dad Frank and world-weary friends Toby and Anna. In the first episode, Geoffrey reluctantly allows Lee to help Lucy clear up the builder's mess in their new kitchen extension. With Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's plans hit a brick wall when solicitor Ritchie is too busy to take on the custody case, so Ben offers to help. Frankie is nervous when the police arrive with a lead on Nancy's case, Chelsea tries to persuade Gray to let her move in - in her own seductive way - and Bobby is flustered when Dana is arrives at the restaurant. The Walford regulars hear a rumour that football legend Harry Redknapp is making an appearance at the Vic for the Euros. Word is, he's a friend of Rocky - but no one believes a word he says

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Highlights of recent events

programme.title: A View from the Euros
programme.desc: The gang members highlight their favourite moments of Euro 2020, looking back on everything they've enjoyed from the past six weeks before falling head over heels for their unexpected heroes in Holiday Romance. Robert Borthwick goes sifting through the social media EuroTrash and the gang decides what moment should be preserved for ever in the Time Capsule. They end by picking their favourites for One Night Only as the tournament reaches the final.

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: Amy Irons and David Farrell chat to singer Jack Savoretti about his new album Europiana, Jodie McCallum talks to singer Tom Odell about filming his new music video in an empty Wembley Stadium, and we chat all the latest Love Island theories with former contestant Amy Hart

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a former commercial unit in Madeley in Staffordshire, a fire-damaged house in Wolverhampton and a former care home in Ilkeston in Derbyshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the controversial killing of a police officer which sparked the round-up of dozens of suspects and ended with three men going to the gallows

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp takes a trip along Pontcysyllte aqueduct

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, featuring action from the outside courts

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer demonstrated why they were still the top two seeds after many years at the pinnacle of the game when they recorded victories over David Goffin and Kei Nishikori respectively at this stage in 2019, and the two eventually met in the final, with Djokovic prevailing

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Given the hospital's chequered history, everyone is anxious and on high alert as the Care Quality Commission conducts an emergency inspection. Josh's mother puts pressure on his age-gap romance with Ange, while doubt is cast on a patient Rich Peterson's claim to have been bitten by a dog

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes more people with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. First through the doors is Graeme with a fairground dodgem that he hopes will bump up the bids from the dealers. Gloria brings in an electric massager that she bought at a car-boot sale, and Jonathan hopes to sell a piece of mysterious motoring memorabilia, which even has auctioneer Simon Bower perplexed

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Toy expert David Burville and the 'teddy bear ladies' Julie and Amanda team up to restore a long-retired Victorian curiosity, a piano-playing doll powered by clockwork. Steve Fletcher needs his mechanical expertise as he works on a vintage bus conductor's ticket machine, Kirsten Ramsay tries to repair a fire-damaged trophy belonging to a former world champion kickboxer and the team are faced with an unusual reminder of a secret agent's wartime record

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are joined by former England star Alex Scott and comedian Jack Whitehall for the last of their live shows, as they react to the concluding semi-final and look forward to the final

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: England v Denmark. Gary Lineker presents action from the second semi-final, as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals met at Wembley Stadium. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium, as the line-up for Sunday's showpiece match was confirmed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit properties in Gillingham in Kent, Chaddesden in Derby and Burslem in Stoke-on-Trent, all of which have different renovation issues to resolve before they can be sold

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1863 in which engine tender James Barton was murdered and his prized silver pocket watch stolen. The family of 30-year-old blacksmith Thomas Grime handed in the pocket watch and when questioned Grime admitted being present when the murder was committed, but accused others of the fatal assault. However, Grime alone was convicted of murder

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain in Shetland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Gary Pe help two teams hunt for items at the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, while Eric Knowles explores the Japanese concept of wabi-sabi in pottery

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 10 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the ladies' singles semi-finals are scheduled take place, featuring Aryna Sabalenka v Karolina Pliskova and Ashleigh Barty v Angelique Kerber. Kerber is the only one of these players to have previously won this title, having defeated Serena Williams in the 2018 final, but any of the four would make a worthy champion given the fine form they have all displayed so far in this year's tournament

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Ronan Keating and Clara Amfo host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is angry when Denise refuses to let him see Raymond again, but refuses to give up without a fight. Lola realises she needs to step up as Isaac returns home, especially as Sheree thinks all her son needs is a few weeks' rest

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.desc: A charming bank robber breaks out of jail and takes a female US Marshal hostage. Steven Soderbergh's romantic crime drama, based on Elmore Leonard's novel, with George Clooney, Jennifer Lopez, Ving Rhames and Don Cheadle

programme.title: Murchadh MacPharlain: Bard Mhealaboist
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary profiling Murdo Macfarlane, the Melbost Bard, the renowned Gaelic poet and songwriter from the island of Lewis, whose work was a catalyst for a whole new era of modern Gaelic music. Having composed poems and songs all his life, it was not until the advent of the tape recorder that Murdo, who could neither read nor write music, became known to a wider audience. The programme contains TV and radio archive recordings of Murdo in interviews, and also of him performing his work

programme.title: Air an Rathad (On the Road)
programme.desc: Donnie Mackay heads to the Grampian Transport Museum in Alford, for a car show with a difference. Linda MacLeod drives BMW's new baby M car, the M2, considering whether it can put the fun factor back into the German manufacturer's arsenal. Plus, the team members learn what lies in store for them in this year's Ralaidh na Trealaich

programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Adam, Nathan, Emma and Rita work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: England v Denmark (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the second semi-final, as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium. The Three Lions have grown into this tournament, topping Group D thanks to efficient if not spectacular 1-0 wins over Croatia and Czech Republic, and a 0-0 draw with Scotland. They then laid to rest some of their past demons with a 2-0 victory over Germany in the last-16, and thrashed Ukraine 4-0 in Rome in the quarter-finals, becoming the first team in the history of the competition to keep five consecutive clean sheets in their first five games. Denmark have bounced back from the brink of elimination to reach the last four, riding a wave of emotion along the way. Influential midfielder Christian Eriksen collapsed on the pitch and suffered a cardiac arrest during their opening match with Finland, which they would go on to lose 1-0. They were then beaten 2-1 by Belgium in their second group match, but came storming

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ryan is pleased when Mack appears. Gabby is left shaken. Bernice gives unconventional marriage guidance counselling

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.desc: The actor explores horses in history, travelling high into the Pyrenees mountains, where there is a record of the horse drawn on the walls of the Niaux Cave from 14,000 years ago. A hundred years ago, fishermen used horses to trawl the sea for shrimps, and Martin spends a day fishing with these last European horse fishermen. And in Dubai he visits the Meydan racecourse, the one place that still connects us with our equine past

programme.title: Diana's Decades
programme.desc: Documentary taking a fresh approach to the life of Diana, Princess of Wales, recounting how she was shaped by the changing world around her and, as a result, transformed both the monarchy and aspects of society. The series tracks down people who met her or whose lives intersected hers in some way, and includes rarely seen archive footage from Diana's life, alongside iconic scenes from the changing social, fashion and economic cultures she lived through during the 1980s and 1990s

programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Adam, Nathan, Emma and Rita work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: England v Denmark (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the second semi-final, as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium. The Three Lions have grown into this tournament, topping Group D thanks to efficient if not spectacular 1-0 wins over Croatia and Czech Republic, and a 0-0 draw with Scotland. They then laid to rest some of their past demons with a 2-0 victory over Germany in the last-16, and thrashed Ukraine 4-0 in Rome in the quarter-finals, becoming the first team in the history of the competition to keep five consecutive clean sheets in their first five games. Denmark have bounced back from the brink of elimination to reach the last four, riding a wave of emotion along the way. Influential midfielder Christian Eriksen collapsed on the pitch and suffered a cardiac arrest during their opening match with Finland, which they would go on to lose 1-0. They were then beaten 2-1 by Belgium in their second group match, but came storming

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.desc: The actor explores horses in history, travelling high into the Pyrenees mountains, where there is a record of the horse drawn on the walls of the Niaux Cave from 14,000 years ago. A hundred years ago, fishermen used horses to trawl the sea for shrimps, and Martin spends a day fishing with these last European horse fishermen. And in Dubai he visits the Meydan racecourse, the one place that still connects us with our equine past

programme.title: Diana's Decades
programme.desc: Documentary taking a fresh approach to the life of Diana, Princess of Wales, recounting how she was shaped by the changing world around her and, as a result, transformed both the monarchy and aspects of society. The series tracks down people who met her or whose lives intersected hers in some way, and includes rarely seen archive footage from Diana's life, alongside iconic scenes from the changing social, fashion and economic cultures she lived through during the 1980s and 1990s

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is busy honeymooning with Jo and in his absence Aidensfield is transformed from a sleepy rural retreat into a scene of mayhem and violence. A pair of armed robbers disguised as hippies take Greengrass hostage during a payroll heist and shoot his beloved dog. Despite the best efforts of new constable Mike Bradley, the villains make their getaway - and Blaketon's heroic intervention causes him to collapse with a heart attack

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Deightons, a family of crooked travellers, pitches camp in Aidensfield, and it is not long before its members are plotting to steal Greengrass's new horse and even kidnapping Ventress. With Nick still away on honeymoon, it is up to PCs Bellamy and Bradley to bring the rogues to justice. Bill Maynard and Derek Fowlds star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice is dreading Christmas. Sarah worries that Andy is brooding about his dad. Roy dresses up as Santa for the kids. Ashley struggles to get the truth from Diane

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is alarmed when she discovers Andy is missing. Kathy asks Graham to spend the night with her. The Sugdens' Christmas Day does not go to plan

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin snaps under the pressure of seeing Sally and Greg together. Jack is horrified at the thought of going on holiday with Vera. Des and Natalie get ready for Colin's wedding

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A schoolgirl is found dead after a disco, and Cornish copper Charles Wycliffe applies himself to the task of discovering whether she was murdered or committed suicide. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: With the estate's future hanging in the balance, Mary hopes to receive a bailout from her grandmother Martha - and with Violet's help, tries to make sure Downton puts on a bigger show than ever before with a grand party for friends and neighbours. Meanwhile, Mrs Hughes hears worrying news, and O'Brien's efforts to boost Alfred's progress meets with disapproval from Thomas. Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Shirley MacLaine and Maggie Smith

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: A birthday weekend away goes devastatingly wrong when Angela and four of her friends travel down from Glasgow to celebrate in Bath. When a rugby player they met during the course of the night turns up dead, they all become suspects as McDonald and Dodds investigate what appears to be a case of murder. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances by Sharon Rooney, John Thomson and Natalie Gumede

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective is drawn into the criminal underworld of Soho when he reluctantly accepts an unofficial request from Chief Superintendent Rydell to find his missing daughter. Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the perpetrator is found dead in his home and when a battered body is found in a local nightclub, the identity of the victim leads Banks and Cabbot to a devastating realisation about both cases

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. A surveillance operation establishes a clear link between the murders of Charles McKay and Barry Clough and the subsequent investigation takes a number of alarming twists before uncovering a conspiracy pointing to one of the team being an informer. As Banks pushes all the suspects hard, the case threatens to undermine his integrity as an officer and brings his relationship with Cabbot to breaking point

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.desc: Nick moves up a rank to Sergeant and Blaketon becomes the new postmaster, while Greengrass threatens revenge on the miscreant responsible for dosing his beloved dog Alfred with rat poison. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day one of the July meeting, including feature the 3.35 Princess of Wales's Stakes, which follows races at 2.05 and 2.25, plus a race at 2.05 from Carlisle. Presented by Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani, with analysis from Jason Weaver and Hayley Turner, reporting by Sally Ann Grassick and Oli Bell, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.desc: Graham tells Kathy he wants to see her more. Pollard and Turner try to impress Diane. Bernice throws Gavin's belongings out

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice's birthday is spoilt when she receives a card from Gavin. Emily asks Viv if she can have her job back. Diane organises a lock-in at the pub just for the girls

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider asks Anne to help him find Curly. Gail worries about Sally investing all her money in Greg. Roy is furious when Mike calls Hayley a freak

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy realises he has ruined everything for Hayley. Natalie agrees to move in with Des. Gary has an audition for a band. Toyah returns from holiday with a boyfriend

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Important comedy milestones of 1998, including The Royle Family and Goodness Gracious Me, and the final episodes of Drop the Dead Donkey and Father Ted. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Edith's big day finally arrives, but will her enthusiasm convince the family that Strallan's age doesn't matter? Meanwhile, Mrs Hughes anxiously awaits her results, Anna makes a breakthrough to help Bates, and Mary takes matters into her own hands to secure the estate's future - but the personal cost could be high. Costume drama, starring Laura Carmichael, Robert Bathurst, Phyllis Logan, Joanne Froggatt and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a seemingly beloved resident of a coastal community is fatally driven off the road, and realises she has to dig up the victim's past to have any chance of unmasking the killer. Joined by her faithful team, including DC Kenny Lockhart and new recruit DC Jac Williams, Vera attempts to find the missing link that will crack the case, but finds it difficult to get to the truth when everyone they encounter seems to be protecting a secret. Crime drama based on Ann Cleeves' series of novels, starring Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective receives a disturbing phone message from estranged brother Roy and heads to Harrogate to find him. Meanwhile at a murder scene, the team is introduced to new recruit Helen Morton, who quickly alienates her colleagues with her tactless style. The officer then makes matters worse when she finds evidence linking Banks to the victim's body and speculates he could be a suspect. Unaware everyone is searching for him, the DCI begins to delve into Roy's life and soon uncovers questionable business transactions and associates. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Caroline Catz (Doc Martin) and Andrea Lowe

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective visits his childhood home to break the news of Roy's death, and his parents plead with him to find out what happened. Defying Morton, Banks returns to the station in an attempt to lead the investigation into the murders of his brother and Jennifer Lewis, and both officers are convinced the key to cracking the cases lies with a woman being treated at a clinic. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass starts a devious plot involving the postal service, much to Blaketon's annoyance, and several people are suspected of attacking a TV repair man. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day two of the July meeting, featuring four races, including the Falmouth Stakes. Plus, two races from York, including the 2.40 Summer Stakes. Presented by Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani, with analysis from Jason Weaver and Hayley Turner, reporting by Sally Ann Grassick and Oli Bell, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gavin tries to talk to Bernice, but she is not interested. Chris tells Zoe about a new business idea. News of Gavin's sexuality spreads around the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Diane warns Ashley to keep his distance from Bernice. Viv gives Terry a roasting for working for the Tates. Bernice turns down Alan's offer to buy the pub

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice worries about Toyah going out with yobbish Dobber. Anne admits to Spider that she set Curly up. Jim takes Liz out for dinner, but it does not go as well as he hoped

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Toyah threatens to run away again if her parents stop her from seeing Dobber. Liz is upset that Jim is still his old aggressive self. Maxine fears Audrey intends to sack her

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look at comedy in 2003, when Tim, Lee and Dawn's love triangle reached a memorable conclusion in The Office and the original run of Cold Feet bowed out with a tragic storyline. It was also the year when members of the public found themselves the unwitting comedy stooges of hidden-camera shows like Trigger Happy TV and Three Non Blondes, Bruce Forsyth made a memorable appearance as guest host of Have I Got News for You and comedy legend Bob Monkhouse performed his last stand-up gig to an audience of fellow comics in a small London pub

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Branson's political views land him in trouble and Sybil's loyalty is tested to the limit, while Ethel makes a difficult decision about her son's future. Anna wonders what is causing Bates' silence, and Carson begins recruiting a footman, with promising candidate Jimmy (Ed Speleers, Eragon) sparking immediate interest below stairs. Costume drama, starring Allen Leech, Jessica Brown Findlay, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt, Brendan Coyle and Jim Carter

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack panics when Vera talks about withdrawing some money for a foreign holiday. Roy and Hayley spend their first night together. Sally threatens to call the police on Kevin

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Hayley wonders if she will ever be able to make Roy happy. Audrey dupes Alf into buying the Salon. Vera is horrified when she hears about Jack's betting losses

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice asks Gavin to leave. Lyn invites Scott to spend Christmas lunch with her. Betty pleads with Reggie not to drag up her past

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv is lonely at Christmas without a man. Diane returns to the village. The Home Farm staff party gets underway. Bernice tells Diane the truth about Gavin

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is busy honeymooning with Jo and in his absence Aidensfield is transformed from a sleepy rural retreat into a scene of mayhem and violence. A pair of armed robbers disguised as hippies take Greengrass hostage during a payroll heist and shoot his beloved dog. Despite the best efforts of new constable Mike Bradley, the villains make their getaway - and Blaketon's heroic intervention causes him to collapse with a heart attack

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Deightons, a family of crooked travellers, pitches camp in Aidensfield, and it is not long before its members are plotting to steal Greengrass's new horse and even kidnapping Ventress. With Nick still away on honeymoon, it is up to PCs Bellamy and Bradley to bring the rogues to justice. Bill Maynard and Derek Fowlds star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice is dreading Christmas. Sarah worries that Andy is brooding about his dad. Roy dresses up as Santa for the kids. Ashley struggles to get the truth from Diane

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is alarmed when she discovers Andy is missing. Kathy asks Graham to spend the night with her. The Sugdens' Christmas Day does not go to plan

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin snaps under the pressure of seeing Sally and Greg together. Jack is horrified at the thought of going on holiday with Vera. Des and Natalie get ready for Colin's wedding

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A schoolgirl is found dead after a disco, and Cornish copper Charles Wycliffe applies himself to the task of discovering whether she was murdered or committed suicide. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: With the estate's future hanging in the balance, Mary hopes to receive a bailout from her grandmother Martha - and with Violet's help, tries to make sure Downton puts on a bigger show than ever before with a grand party for friends and neighbours. Meanwhile, Mrs Hughes hears worrying news, and O'Brien's efforts to boost Alfred's progress meets with disapproval from Thomas. Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Shirley MacLaine and Maggie Smith

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: A birthday weekend away goes devastatingly wrong when Angela and four of her friends travel down from Glasgow to celebrate in Bath. When a rugby player they met during the course of the night turns up dead, they all become suspects as McDonald and Dodds investigate what appears to be a case of murder. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances by Sharon Rooney, John Thomson and Natalie Gumede

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective is drawn into the criminal underworld of Soho when he reluctantly accepts an unofficial request from Chief Superintendent Rydell to find his missing daughter. Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the perpetrator is found dead in his home and when a battered body is found in a local nightclub, the identity of the victim leads Banks and Cabbot to a devastating realisation about both cases

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. A surveillance operation establishes a clear link between the murders of Charles McKay and Barry Clough and the subsequent investigation takes a number of alarming twists before uncovering a conspiracy pointing to one of the team being an informer. As Banks pushes all the suspects hard, the case threatens to undermine his integrity as an officer and brings his relationship with Cabbot to breaking point

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur decides he wants to retire and Terry helps him get out of a deal with a dubious consortium of Masonic property developers. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Counterfeiters lead Simon a merry dance from London to Switzerland via Paris, and the involvement of a bogus countess only complicates matters further. Roger Moore stars, with Kate O'Mara and Philip Madoc

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed finds himself prime suspect when foreign agents are suddenly systematically killed off, so initiates a truce with his Russian opposite and together they work to discover the identity of the killers

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle and Bodie pose as hitmen to infiltrate a mysterious group of hire killers whose targets are all retired government officials, and supposedly no longer of any strategic importance. Adventure, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A sudden staffing crisis leaves Arthur with no choice but to employ his nephew Ray to help him sell cut-price cars from Brussels - his idea of entering the European market. Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of sports covered by ITV during the 1980s, ranging from football and boxing to wrestling and double decker racing

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 10 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 191km course from Albertville to Valence. programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade responds to reports from one of the US east coast's most popular vacation destinations, the sunny Florida Keys. A creature is attacking both visitors and locals alike, striking its victims so violently it is slicing open flesh and even smashing bones, and the assaults are happening both above and below the water. Jeremy is determined to discover the identity of the assailant and uncover what is triggering these attacks. He knows it is not going to be easy, and it is not long before the intrepid marine biologist fears he may be out of his depth

programme.title: The Untouchables (1987)
programme.desc: Incorruptible federal agent Eliot Ness is assigned the task of fighting organised crime in Prohibition-era Chicago and bringing the notorious crime boss Al Capone to justice. Brian De Palma's crime thriller, with Kevin Costner, Robert De Niro, Andy Garcia and Sean Connery, who won an Oscar for his performance

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A bloody attack on a young swimmer points to something dangerous in a remote stretch of the Parana River in Argentina. Jeremy Wade discovers locals are blaming a creature they call the River Tiger - a muscular predator with jaws like a bolt cutter - but to back up their stories, the freshwater detective needs to catch a specimen big enough to inflict the chilling wounds he has heard about

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Beyond the Amazon, in the remote jungles of Guyana, fishermen are disappearing without trace. Locals blame the 'water mama', a mermaid that is said to drag victims to their death. Could the largely unexplored rivers of this region be hiding a new monster, or is there a more familiar predator lurking beneath the surface? When an eyewitness claims to have seen the creature in a lake, Jeremy Wade's hunt for this mysterious bodysnatcher takes an unexpected turn, bringing him face to face with an astonishing beast

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole reveals the Norton redesign to Norton boss Stuart Garner, before testing the CCM Spitfire

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 12 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 159.5km route from Saint-Paul-Trois-Chateaux to Nimes. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali, Dovey and Spooner head to the north east of France near to Reims, to Crete Lakes - a prestigious and well-known water that holds colossal carp up to an incredible 85lbs. The challenge here is for Dovey to catch a long overdue 70lb carp - If he fails, he must jump in the icy cold lake, but if he succeeds, then Ali and Spoons will have to dive in themselves

programme.title: The Living Daylights (1987)
programme.desc: James Bond crosses the continents to help a KGB agent to defect to the West. While protecting him from an unknown assassin, the super-spy is drawn into the world of arms dealing and a plot to trade millions of pounds' worth of diamonds for weapons to supply mercenaries around the world. Action adventure, with Timothy Dalton making his debut appearance as 007, alongside Maryam d'Abo, Joe Don Baker and Art Malik

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In Fiji's South Pacific paradise, people are coming under attack. programme.title: River Monsters
