programme.desc: Married couple Cliff Walker and Angela Adkin from Nottingham fell in love with St Lucia when they were on a Caribbean cruise, and now, after discovering their pensions aren't performing as well as they'd like, they've decided to invest in a property there. Can Jasmine Harman find a place that will be a sound investment and also give them and their family an idyllic holiday home for years to come, all for their £120,000 budget?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Childhood sweethearts Debbie and Tony Parkes have been married for 40 years, and are ready for a new chapter in their lives. With retirement around the corner, they've set their hearts on a holiday home in Albufeira on Portugal's Algarve with a budget of £80,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Salesman Richard Massey hosts the first party in Colchester, hoping his cheeky-chappy demeanour and signature dish Drunken Duck will be enough to see him walk away with the £1,000 prize at the end of the week-long competition

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PR worker Darius G Laws hosts the second dinner party in Colchester, but knows he will have to work hard after being critical the previous evening. He creates a menu filled with local ingredients and home-grown produce, including his own blackberry wine, and hopes his Roman dress code will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: American army wife Adrena Austin hosts the third dinner party in Colchester. She hopes her simple menu, which revisits her Armenian heritage, will be a welcome change for her guests, but while reactions to the main course are mixed, her entertainment proves a resounding success

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: James Coe throws the fourth party in Colchester, and his choice of menu proves popular from the starter onwards. However, Jodie Ezekiel finds his incessant discussion of money somewhat off-putting. Fortunately, the prospect of a dip in the host's hot tub improves moods, and gives Darius G Laws a chance to show off his pink pants

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Jodie Edzekiel hosts the final dinner party in Colchester, and tries to please her guests by serving them meat, provided she can bring herself to touch it. When Jodie's planned entertainment lets her down, she hopes a sing-a-long with her accompanying on the organ will save the day

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in New Jersey to help Rose Leone, who spent two years in a coma and came out of hospital to learn her son Michael had run her once-thriving Italian restaurant into the ground. With a menu of more than 126 dishes, the quality of the food is suffering, and even the chefs refuse to eat their own creations

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Ben and Billie continue to create their riverside glamping area by raising the roof of their dilapidated boathouse, while it's all hands on deck as Amy and Marc aim to complete their barn and cafe in time for hosting their first yoga class and celebratory meal

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karl and Naomh, who first met at university, love the relaxed lifestyle of Menorca and want to be near the beautiful beaches of its south-east coast where they held their wedding. With Scarlette Douglas on hand and a top cash budget of £230,000, they are looking for a place with plenty of outside space for themselves and their two dogs

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps Teresa Lynch search for a property in the western Algarve, Portugal. With dramatically falling house prices, she hopes her budget of £120,000 will be enough to fulfil her lifelong dream of living on the country's coast

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles bring together the three best teams from both sets of heats, whose face challenge is to create 24 perfectly identical almond pithivier pies and individual savoury brioche, all in just three and a half hours. There, judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden ask them to produce a striking sugar showpiece, inspired by a futuristic jungle theme, along with two types of confectionery of their choice

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: A steam engine enthusiast embarks on an ambitious coast-to-coast journey across Devon, travelling across the county's rolling hills in an 80-year-old steamroller with a top speed of eight miles per hour. Farmers on Dartmoor rear a rare breed of sheep, while at Falmouth, a group of free divers explores a spectacular underwater cave

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on Northamptonshire police's tackling of hate crime, following PCs Lauren El Sharkawi and Nina Crehan after they investigate a call from a family in Northampton who have been subjected to racist abuse from a neighbour. The police are also experiencing a surge in homophobic and transphobic abuse and are called to deal with one incident where the victim being targeted is just 13

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedic Rob Armstrong and technician Mike Horton receive a call from a woman at a hotel who is about to give birth. They rush through traffic as they try to get her to hospital before the baby is born in the back of the ambulance. In Birmingham, paramedic Rob Moore and technician Chloe Walker treat a two-year-old boy who is struggling to breathe, and in Coventry, paramedics Floss Corcoran and Orlaigh Barber help an elderly man who has suffered a blow to the head

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more singletons to choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. Six-foot pansexual opera singer Clare is looking for a man as tall as she is, while Jessica, who has a disastrous dating history, hopes to bring out her naughty side

programme.title: Kathy Burke: Money Talks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring people's relationship with money, in which the actor, writer and director travels the country to meet people at both extremes of the wealth spectrum. In the first episode, she reunited with former comedy partners Harry Enfield and Paul Whitehouse to discuss creating characters defined by wealth and status, and meets a wealthy businessman who grew up in poverty as a Romany gypsy,

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Nathan and Amye as they build a giant cathedral-like property modelled on Dutch barn-style houses, but with a contemporary twist. It is a hugely ambitious plan, and with Nathan project managing and setting themselves just a year to finish the build, the pressure to pull it off is huge

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth steer a 72-foot vessel along the waterways of Staffordshire, journeying along the Caldon Canal and the Trent and Mersey Canal, to Stoke-on-Trent. They begin their journey at Cheddleton Flint Mill, built in 1253, hearing why flint was such an essential pottery ingredient. Gyles pays tribute to one of his great heroes, the Potteries-born writer Arnold Bennett, and Sheila visits the former home of one of her heroes, the ceramic artist Clarice Cliff

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart gets into trouble with the police for vandalising Flanders' Beatles memorabilia, so Marge sends him on an organised activity scheme to make sure he is supervised at all times. Unfortunately, Homer is appointed team leader, and it is not long before things start to fall apart

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Animal lover Rachel Imms hosts the fourth party of the week from Mansfield. She opts for a French-themed evening, but her complicated main course proves to be a challenge, and her accordionist entertainer does not appeal to everyone in the way she had hoped

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Contestants from Cardiff and Newport compete to host the best dinner party and win £1,000 cash prize. programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Allan have been together for 10 years and with three grown up children between them, are hoping to buy their first home together - somewhere they can go to relax and unwind in Manilva, Spain. programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fay Lewis has dreamed of moving to Spain for 20 years. Now, with her house in the UK sold and her bags packed, she's ready to take the plunge. Jasmine Harman shows Fay five properties around the Axarquia region of the Costa del Sol for her budget of 95,000 euros

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton searches for a retirement home on Spain's Costa de Almeria for Ted and Diane Brewer, who met on holiday 20 years ago and would now like to live in the sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Banker Mandeep Rajput is keen to impress as he hosts the first dinner party in Dudley, West Midlands, joined by musical pensioner Di Dawson, quiz master Adam Reece, dog groomer Vicky Pell and businesswoman Sarah Freeman

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pub quiz host Adam Reece takes charge of the second dinner party in Dudley, West Midlands, hoping his 'mixed up' theme, Italian menu and game show-style entertainment will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pensioner Di Dawson hosts the third dinner party from Dudley, West Midlands, and hopes some good honest home cooking will impress the guests. However, the evening is overshadowed by Adam Reece's offensive humour and his flirtatious behaviour with dog groomer Vicky Pell

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog groomer Vicky Pell hosts a cruise ship-themed party for her guests in Dudley in the West Midlands, but Adam, whose smutty sense of humour has caused outrage all week, continues to behave badly. His actions eventually create a rift with Di, threatening to turn the evening sour

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businesswoman Sarah Freeman hosts the final dinner party from Dudley, West Midlands, and hopes to impress her guests with a Moroccan menu, but the simmering tension between rivals Adam Reece and Di Dawson threatens to jeopardise her chances of winning the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: First of a two-part special in which Gordon Ramsay heads to the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions are running high because owner Alan Saffron has spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. To make matters worse, the kitchen is run by a chef called David Blaine who shares nothing of his famous namesake's magic when it comes to cooking - and whose threatening behaviour regularly gets him into trouble

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge joins Elaine and David as they look around an 1850s chateau with 25 rooms, telling them what to look out for when buying such a vast property. The programme also catches up with Tim and Krys from the first series, who renovated rooms for a boutique B&B in the Dordogne, but have been struggling to juggle parenthood with running the business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Teresa, who was born in Portugal but moved to the UK when she was five years old, find a holiday home in the Algarve to reconnect with her heritage

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul and Bev have been married for a decade and now wish to realise their dream of relocating to Torrevieja, Spain. With a maximum budget of £100,000 and a long list of family and friends ready to come out and visit, this extremely sociable pair want a property with enough space for guests as well as a music room for musician Paul. Jean Johansson sets out to help find them an appropriate property

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at 20 years of helping house-hunters find homes with character. Chris and Louise want a statement property in Cambridgeshire, but even with a budget of £800,000, it's proving hard to find. A Victorian folly is Colin and Robin's dream, Amy is proving fussy as she and boyfriend Matt search for a city centre flat in Edinburgh, and Pip is searching for a chocolate box cottage in Cheshire and north Wales, with space for chickens and a home office

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 56-year-old woman is rushed to St George's with an open ankle fracture after falling in her garden, and a man suffering chest pains looks back on his career on the railway, which was cut short by a leg amputation. An 84-year-old man collapses at home following a bout of dizziness, and his children reflect on the challenges of watching his health decline in recent years

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedic Rob Armstrong and technician Mike Horton receive a call from a woman at a hotel who is about to give birth. They rush through traffic as they try to get her to hospital before the baby is born in the back of the ambulance. In Birmingham, paramedic Rob Moore and technician Chloe Walker treat a two-year-old boy who is struggling to breathe, and in Coventry, paramedics Floss Corcoran and Orlaigh Barber help an elderly man who has suffered a blow to the head

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Rogue Nation (2015)
programme.desc: The team of secret agents faces being shut down when the head of the CIA convinces the US Senate that it is too dangerous. The operatives of the Impossible Mission Force become fugitives when they defy orders and plot to bring down a shadowy society of assassins. Spy thriller sequel, starring Tom Cruise, Jeremy Renner, Rebecca Ferguson and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more singletons to choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. Six-foot pansexual opera singer Clare is looking for a man as tall as she is, while Jessica, who has a disastrous dating history, hopes to bring out her naughty side

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary looking back at some of some of Noel Fitzpatrick's most memorable cases and catching up with the animals at the heart of the stories. Up first are puppies, including four-month-old West Highland terrier Rodney, who had been born without back feet, bulldog puppy Chica, who required urgent surgery to repair a serious elbow fracture, and Rottweiler puppy Elvis, who was given a unique spinal implant developed by Noel

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help more families across the UK solve their design and DIY dilemmas

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dance fanatic Julia Smith hosts the final party in Mansfield, Nottinghamshire, and is confident she will wow fellow competitors with her fancy house and hospitality, having roped in two waiters to give the evening a touch of class. A spot of salsa dancing proves entertaining for all the wrong reasons, and as the event draws to a close, the contestants gather to discover which dinner was rated the best

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from south Wales compete, beginning with Esther and Darryl, who fight for control in the kitchen. Next up are Louise and David, who aim to impress with their Welsh-themed evening, and give a revealing insight into their private lives. The contestants expect an extravagant party on the final night, which is hosted by Carl and Brian, before the winners of the £1,000 prize are announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton is on the Costa Calida in Spain, with Nadine, who is looking to permanently relocate to Spain with her husband Tyler. Nadine's best friend Jannette is coming on the search to offer a helping hand as shows the friends five properties within the budget of £160,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tina Watkins has long struggled to get on the housing ladder in the UK. Now, she has decided to make the most of the incredibly good value property in Spain's Granada province and buy her first-ever home. With her best friend Jamie along to help, Jasmine Harman shows Tina just how far her £19,000 budget will stretch

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Angie and Sandy Stanford-Barclay from Nottingham are approaching retirement and looking for a place where they can spend quality time together. They've decided the sunshine of the southern Costa Blanca is the perfect place to do just that, so Ben Hillman shows them five properties for their £75,000 budget

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Oil and gas co-ordinator Danielle Greenwood is keen to impress as she hosts the first dinner party in the Merseyside area, as she welcomes divorcee Jade Chantelle Short, Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers, pastoral care manager Steven Booth, and nursery school teacher Anna Senior into her home. Danielle takes no risks with a down-to-earth menu, which she hopes will help bag her the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pastoral care manager Steven Booth hosts the second Merseyside dinner party, and names each course after a Tina Turner song, hoping to win the £1,000 prize. The food is well received, but tensions rise when one guest makes a tactless comment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glamorous PR representative Jade Chantelle hosts the third night in Merseyside, where she hopes a casino-themed evening with lashings of champagne and a high-class menu will help her to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Primary school teacher Anna Senior hosts the fourth night in Merseyside with a fairy tale-themed evening. Her menu consists of a Mad Hatter's soup and sandwich, a special pork dish cooked three ways and baked cheesecake for dessert, but she must try to control the tension between Jamie and Jade to have a successful party

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Michael Bublé impersonator Jamie Somers hosts the final Merseyside dinner party, but may have to put aside his differences with guest Jade if he is to be crowned the winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part two of two. The chef continues his quest to transform the fortunes of the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions were running high because owner Alan Saffron had spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim battles to get a fence built for their marauding flock of 25 sheep, and Krys battles to balance the books. Elaine and David continue the search for their dream property, visiting a 17th-century ex-priory in Normandy and taking on board all that Dick has taught them

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a young man who bought a 70-year-old boat for £5,000 that he intends to turn into a home, and to find a shipmate to help with the restoration, he turns to an internet dating site. There is also a cricket-mad grandad building a mini pavilion in his garden, with the 1950s-inspired build incorporating bats, bails and even a boundary rope alongside an ingenious bunk bed that with the pull of a chord transforms into a sofa. George's German road trip reaches Berlin, and a house situated where the Wall once stood

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a man planning to build Margate's first Victorian bathing machine in more than 100 years, and a coma survivor who proves he has a sense of humour by transforming a hearse into a family camper van. The presenter also visits a woman who has turned her narrowboat home into a floating bookshop, and in Germany he sees a lifeguard tower straight out of a 1950s sci-fi comic

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at 20 years of helping house-hunters find homes with character. Chris and Louise want a statement property in Cambridgeshire, but even with a budget of £800,000, it's proving hard to find. Six-foot pansexual opera singer Clare is looking for a man as tall as she is, while Jessica, who has a disastrous dating history, hopes to bring out her naughty side

programme.title: Sex Party Secrets
programme.desc: As the popularity of exclusive arranged orgies rises, this documentary explores the hidden world of the UK's self-proclaimed 'sexual elite', providing an insight into the lives of the most selective party planners and their meticulously vetted clients. As the guests sip champagne and fulfil their wildest fantasies behind the doors of multimillion-pound mansions and country estates, the programme explores the motivation behind the desire that drives married couples, young single friends and seasoned sex addicts to seek fleeting intimate experiences and group encounters with strangers

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles bring together the three best teams from both sets of heats, whose face challenge is to create 24 perfectly identical almond pithivier pies and individual savoury brioche, all in just three and a half hours. There, judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden ask them to produce a striking sugar showpiece, inspired by a futuristic jungle theme, along with two types of confectionery of their choice

programme.desc: Maggie becomes obsessed with unlikely babysitter Moe - much to the dismay of Marge and Homer, who think he is an unsuitable role model and ban him from seeing her again

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Expats Graham and Linda hope want to open a takeaway in Benalmadena which specialises in selling pies, but their dream has been put on hold until the council grants them permission. Will it all prove to be pie in the sky? Caravan site boss Bambi and her neighbour Johnny, who is also her former dance partner, try to rekindle their old magic at the campsite charity fete. Taffy and Leanne have set up their own pool maintenance business - but it's their own pool that is causing them the biggest headache

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Hansen and Gittings families swap homes and budgets for the week - and although they both live in Bournemouth, their financial circumstances differ greatly. Yoko and Richard Hansen live in a five-bedroom house with their one child, take up to four overseas holidays a year and enjoy regular trips to see West End musicals. Their weekly budget is more than £1,300. Sarah and Greg Gittings, meanwhile, live in a two-bedroom flat, and get by on just £116 a week

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: The team are facing one of the busiest days in Barnsley Casualty's history, and the pressure mounts on staff members as patients await beds to become available, so self-named Emergency Clown Sister Jane helps to give the younger nurses a moral boost during this difficult period. Meanwhile, consultant doctor Jane treats 10-year-old Oliver, who suffers from a rare condition called Angelman Syndrome

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers deal with a four-car collision outside a primary school. They make sure all those injured or hurt are accounted for, and also set about finding out who was to blame. A speeding driver is desperate to escape from police and smashes into traffic at a junction, causing carnage before escaping on foot. He has left his passengers behind, but will they help the cops or hinder the course of the investigation? Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire officers are called out to a massive crash near a theme park that has resulted in the death of at least one person. The need to understand what has happened and who - if anyone - is to blame tests the resources of all the traffic cops who attend and then investigate this fatal incident. Will their gut instincts that this is a drug-fuelled disaster prove correct?

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The dog unit embarks on a desperate manhunt amid fears that an armed and dangerous character is roaming the streets of Lincoln, while Craggy closes in on a suspected shoplifter with a bag full of surprises. Officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit also tackle a would-be stunt rider and a boozed-up biker who is raising hell on the A-roads

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A case of drink-driving leads to a hot pursuit on the city streets and carnage on the country roads, while a routine stop for speeding gives a lone female officer a worrying surprise. As devastating storms batter the UK, the helicopter crew is called out to rescue a woman trapped by deadly rising river water

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sgt Jon Moon and his police dog Kanto follow a trail of broken glass and spilt alcohol, leading to a confrontation with a robber wielding a nasty-looking knife. Elsewhere, a fight in a club ends with one man in handcuffs and another in hospital, Barry Peach plays catch-up as a teenage driver tears up the streets of Skegness, and Jon Peacock detects a whiff of cannabis in a quiet rural village

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall trains a German shepherd anxious about her puppy, a bulldog that is always stealing food and faces a particular challenge with a picnic, and a boxer that is driving a wedge between its owners

programme.desc: A collection of internet-sourced videos with a family friendly hook, featuring laugh-out-loud unexpected personal moments now being shared with the world

programme.desc: Graeme Hall takes on a cocker spaniel that loves to steal food and people's belongings, and a very territorial Jack Russell that lashes out at anyone straying too close to areas he has claimed as his own. programme.desc: Romany Gypsy Lee Hughes devises a plan to expand his business by tapping into a lucrative market - treating racehorses. programme.desc: The specialist clinic opens its doors to patients with acne, psoriasis and cysts.

programme.desc: Part two of two. The stories of multimillionaire celebrity hair stylist Nicky Clarke, who unexpectedly found love with his PA Kelly Simpkin, and 72-year-old millionaire Lyn, who is accustomed to dating men 40 years her junior and is on the hunting for Mr Right

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Thirty-eight-year-old Lesley is rushed in when her heart stops while she is driving to work, leaving Dr Morris battle to resuscitate her and investigate why a normally fit and well woman with no known medical problems would suffer such an attack. Abigail, aged 20, is treated for a painful neck injury caused by a riding fall, and 28-year-old Laura is brought in having a severe asthma attack. It is clear to Dr Fawcett she is deteriorating fast and that her heart could stop at any moment

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: The hospital struggles with a huge backlog of untreated patients from the night shift, as staff have a hard time ensuring that everyone is discharged or admitted within four hours of arrival. A woman who tripped while shopping fears that a broken ankle will jeopardise her plans to go on a cruise

programme.desc: Two expats buy the bar they discovered on their honeymoon 21 years before, while a tattooist moonlights as both a reggae singer and a DIY visual artist. A Scottish couple bought a bargain Segway business, but making it work takes a lot of hard graft

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Llewellyn family from Enfield swap homes, budgets and lives with the Hornans from Rickmansworth, and although they live just 20 miles away from each other, their financial circumstances are worlds apart. The Hornans have a lifestyle that many would envy, with good jobs, a large four-bedroom house, annual holidays to Italy and a weekly budget of about £2,300. The Llewellyns, meanwhile, live in a three-bedroom maisonette and get by on just £97 a week

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Barnsley Hospital treats the largest number of patients in a single shift in its history, including a man being rushed in with a suspected brain injury. Health care assistant Sharon Bailey and volunteer Jane Allen try to keep their colleagues' spirits up with tea and cake, while a nine-year-old girl in the paediatric triage area does not want to stay overnight and a strange smell emerges from the lost property box

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents an edition focusing on the hunt for disqualified, unlicensed and uninsured drivers. In Bradford, officers from the West Yorkshire Safer Roads team pursue a desperate and disqualified driver through the city streets, while a member of the public is forced into making a citizen's arrest when another banned motorist attempts to flee the scene of a crash. In York, a pensioner is knocked down in a hit-and-run incident and an urgent search for the culprit gets under way

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Some sleuthing from the road crime team in North Yorkshire uncovers a kitchen where forgers are turning out Scottish £20 notes, while equally dogged investigating leads officers to a man using multiple fake number plates across his fleet of vehicles in an attempt to avoid detection of his numerous offences. Meanwhile, a classic high-speed pursuit and tactical stop on the motorway is undertaken to catch criminals who have stolen a boiler from a plumber's merchant

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers step in to deal with a rowdy reveller when things get out of hand on New Year's Eve in Lincoln city centre, while Suzie Ranyard catches out a motorist who claims he has been driving with a dodgy number plate for 18 years. Jon Moon arrests a shoplifter more than happy to admit to his crime and a young lad discovers how hard it is to pull the wool over the eyes of Jon Peacock

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Brian Conville gives a man a dressing-down for dangerous driving on the school run, and Barry Peach's detective skills come to the fore when he apprehends a fugitive who has been using a false identity. Meanwhile, during an initiative to clamp down on rural crime, officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit find themselves pursuing some stolen tractors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon Moon and his dog Kanto are hot on the heels of some burglary suspects, while spot-checks by the unit on the A1 turn up a carrier bag full of cash. Jason Baxter rushes to the scene of an accident where the driver was thrown clear of the car, only for another crash to take place - this time involving a line of vehicles

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall treats a rescue dog that steals food and barks at the front door - but finds the training techniques for one problem do not work quite as well with the other. Elsewhere, a Jack Russell with strong reactions to traffic makes going for a walk a potentially lethal experience, and two akitas have an aggressive dislike of all other dogs, which the owners fear may turn violent

programme.desc: A crown green bowling team in Saydo is determined to end a losing streak, while Joe and Jan receive some devastating news concerning the future of the dog shelter. A Peterborough man who relocated to the Costa Del Sol launches his mobile cocktail bar, and a former racehorse that has been repeatedly rehomed and returned to the rescue centre may finally have found the right owner

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A toddler is rushed in with suspected sepsis, a life-threatening condition that attacks the organs, and a large team of specialists administer high-strength antibiotics. programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: A bass player has a lump on his forehead that gives him a perpetual headache. Dr Emma operates on a chest cyst, and Dr Adil debates if he can remove a keloid from a patient's ear

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: A woman collapses unexpectedly during a day out with her family to an adventure park, and is left unable to move or walk. The trauma team leader diagnoses a bleed on her brain that requires urgent surgery. A neurosurgeon attempts to drain the build-up of fluid in her brain. A motorist suffers a cardiac arrest behind the wheel, caused by a large clot in an artery, and a van driver is airlifted to hospital after a major traffic collision

programme.title: Extraordinary People
programme.desc: Mandy Sellars from Accrington in Lancashire has an extremely rare condition that has led to the abnormal growth of her legs. In 2010, she had blood poisoning in her left leg and had to have the limb amputated, the side-effects of which resulted in her stump and right leg becoming three stone heavier. This documentary follows endocrinologist Dr Robert Semple and his team at Addenbrooke's Hospital in Cambridge as they develop medication to prevent Mandy's rogue gene from causing her limbs to overgrow

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: The documentary explores the lives of straight sex workers who have turned their heterosexual predilections upside down to perform in gay roles for money. A cammer and porn star reveal what it takes to sell sex to fellow performers of the same gender, despite being straight in their private lives

programme.desc: Police officers monitor events as thousands of protesters wearing V for Vendetta masks march to the Houses of Parliament

Scotsman Graham has hit upon the idea of selling organised pub crawls, while another Graham - the drivetime DJ at Fresh Radio - faces a dilemma when he is offered his old job back, which would mean spending less time with girlfriend Jackie

The Emmotts live in a four-bedroom house in Epsom. They enjoy various activities, while their sons are into sports and music. Their weekly budget is more than £1,300. Single mum Niki Blake, meanwhile, lives in a small flat in Addleston with her children Mia and JJ. After bills, they get by on just £125 a week

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A critically ill patient is brought into the emergency department after suffering a sudden cardiac arrest while in a local cafe with his wife. Meanwhile, doctor Bharath Reddy treats Maxine, who broke her wrist when she slipped de-icing the car, and another patient is treated after fracturing her shoulder

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In Bradford, a stolen car speeds at 100mph through the city as police officers track and pursue it, then forcibly stop the delinquent driver. In Leeds, a car grabs the attention of cops because where they expect to see a middle-aged woman driving, there is instead a teenager

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire's road crime team chases a gang stealing 4x4s from industrial estates every night, while West Yorkshire traffic cops pursue stolen vehicles across Bradford. In Leeds, a 19-year-old risks running across a busy road to meet his girlfriend and the resulting incident requires the full range of emergency services - including an air ambulance - to be deployed to try to save his life

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Barry Peach outsmarts a disqualified driver, handler Jon Peacock takes his new sniffer dog Jack to carry out a drugs warrant, Carl Warrener makes a jaw-dropping discovery when he pulls a car over in the early hours and Brian Conville gets mobbed when he deals with a reckless learner driver

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Barry Peach hunts for a man wanted for assault, officers are hot on the heels of a suspected drug dealer, a drunken driver tries every trick in the book to avoid giving a breath sample and dog-handler Jon Peacock has a late-night encounter with two unusual law-breakers

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan Brigginshaw is kept on his toes when a suspect does a runner, a routine vehicle check turns into a drug bust for Suzie Ranyard, and Barry Peach has his hands full when a group of Scottish revellers clash with bouncers in Skegness. Elsewhere, Jim Woolerton and Jason Baxter catch out a rookie rider who is breaking the law

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall helps to train two dogs that go overboard in protecting the family home and possessions, and another that is particularly protective of the owners' 18-month-old baby. Another candidate for retraining is an energetic cavapoochon who loves to run faster than its owners can handle and does not know to respond to its own name

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Australian soap opera, charting the lives and loves of the residents of the suburb of Erinsborough

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: One inventive dad thinks he has a solution to reduce the amount of accidents outside of schools, but will his pop-up zebra crossing make the streets around his children's school a safer place? Elsewhere in the programme, cameras join the bus drivers of Bath, whose routes are peppered with evidence of careless parking

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits in Blackpool
programme.desc: Documentary following Britons living the high life on a low budget in one of the country's favourite resorts, from holiday-makers to those who have made the Lancashire town their home. Entertainer Simon Green - aka Betty Legs Diamond - talks about his life as a drag queen, and Scottish sisters Agnes and Margaret get more than they bargained for as part of their holiday package. Meanwhile, Jamie Diggins, who works as an estate agent for caravans, takes delivery of a static home that costs an amazing £280,000

programme.title: The Rock (1996)
programme.desc: A renegade general and his crack team of mercenaries take over the abandoned fortress prison of Alcatraz and threaten San Francisco with toxic nerve-gas rockets to extort a massive ransom. To break their stranglehold on the city, the FBI sends in a chemical weapons expert and a jailed SAS veteran, chosen because he is the only man to have ever escaped from the infamous penitentiary. Action thriller, with Sean Connery, Nicolas Cage and Ed Harris

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist talks to Primal Scream singer Bobby Gillespie, who discusses his time in the Jesus and Mary Chain and the records that fuelled his love of music

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: Former Guns N' Roses star Slash talks to Ronnie about his career in rock music and selects some of his favourite records, as well as discussing his lifelong enthusiasm for guitars

programme.title: Trottie True (1949)
programme.desc: A strong-willed showgirl marries into the aristocracy after a fling with a pioneering balloonist - but comes to regret her choice. Period comedy, with Jean Kent and James Donald

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring Wapping for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Once a Soldier
programme.desc: Open for over 300 years and home to 320 old soldiers, the Royal Hospital in Chelsea is under threat. This series follows the residents for a year of their lives

programme.title: London Live News
programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital

programme.title: Taste of Marley
programme.desc: Rohan Marley, son of legendary musician Bob Marley, reconnects with his roots as he goes on a journey to explore the food and music of his native Jamaica

programme.title: Hatton Garden: The Inside Story
programme.desc: Ross Kemp examines the April 2015 heist in London's diamond district, when a group of career criminals broke into a safe deposit facility and stole approximately £14million in precious metals, cash and jewels, an event dubbed the biggest burglary of its type in British history. The documentary gains access to the secret surveillance material that put the thieves behind bars and features exclusive interviews with the detectives who cracked the case as well as Kenny Collins, one of the thieves involved

programme.title: Glam Metal Detectives
programme.desc: Comedy sketch show from 1995, following the oddball characters of the rock band, Glam Metal Detective. Starring Gary Beadle and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Brockley, Peckham and Rotherhithe. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Steve accidentally slices off his finger while using a circular saw, causing all-out panic in the shop, while Mike's girlfriend Anne does the unthinkable and goes shopping at a DIY superstore. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman, Ken Morley, Ryan Cartwright, Peter Serafinowicz and Susan Earl

programme.title: Sex Drugs and HIV
programme.desc: Documentary following an album put together by Mat Sargent in 1995 in support of for HIV charities, featuring over 200 guest musicians and 40 original songs

programme.title: Kurt and Courtney (1997)
programme.desc: Nick Broomfield delves into the life and death of Kurt Cobain, considering the conspiracy theories surrounding his apparent suicide and the West Coast rock scene. It features a dramatic confrontation with Cobain's widow Courtney Love

programme.title: Bloodbath at the House of Death (1983)
programme.desc: Six scientists visit a country house that was once the site of a massacre to investigate psychic disturbances. They do not realise a short-tempered cult leader and his following of inept satanists have taken up residence, and intend to make sure no one leaves the estate alive. Horror spoof, starring Kenny Everett, Vincent Price and Pamela Stephenson

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: A drunk John Hurt arrives in the neighbourhood, while Joe takes advantage of Michael's absence by taking over his house. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: News Rewind
programme.desc: A digest of news and events from across London from the past few days

programme.title: The Most Dangerous Ways to School
programme.desc: Children who take treacherous journeys to school, climbing up mountainous paths, swimming across rivers or fighting their way through icy wastelands

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Wilkes takes her small herd of dairy cows to compete at the Great Yorkshire Show in Harrogate, and Adrian Edmondson meets Tom Orde-Powlett, who is the heir to the historic Bolton Castle in Wensleydale. Plus, 16-year-old Phillip Mellin tries to shear sheep for the first time

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: Tom Orde-Powlett talks about his plans for Bolton Castle, which include reintroducing falconry displays and training an owl to deliver wedding rings to couples getting married there. Shepherdess Amanda Owen enlists the help of her five children to rid her farm of moles, while volunteers and staff at the Wensleydale railway prepare and unload a steam train for an annual celebration

programme.title: The Food Inspectors
programme.desc: Sharon, from West Yorkshire, reveals how she suffered a serious case of salmonella poisoning - resulting in her being admitted to hospital with kidney failure - while Kent animal lover Linda discovers how much bacteria is lurking in her kitchen as a result of giving her pets the run of her home. Chris Hollins heads to Greater Manchester where he visits a Wigan pie-making factory, to witness how safety and hygiene levels are being maintained at every step of the process. Plus, a Chinese takeaway and a pizza delivery company are visited by the food inspectors

programme.title: Henry V (1944)
programme.desc: The young king of England renounces his wild past and rallies the nation behind him as he reasserts his claim on French lands by divine right of kings - driving the two nations toward a climactic confrontation on the battlefield at Agincourt. Shakespeare's historical drama, directed by and starring Laurence Olivier. With Robert Newton, Leslie Banks and Esmond Knight

programme.title: The Edgar Wallace Mystery Theatre
programme.desc: Two men visit a gambler - each with a reason to kill him

programme.title: The Fixers
programme.desc: Four passionate builders, in ten remote locations, have one mission: to improve lives. They will do whatever it takes-saving the world, one build at a time

programme.title: London Live News
programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital

programme.title: Goodnight Sweetheart
programme.desc: Gary realises life in the 1990s does not hold much for him, and decides he would be better off returning to 1941 for good - but soon discovers a daily diet of air-raids, rationing and George Formby on the wireless leave a lot to be desired. Time-travelling romantic comedy, with Nicholas Lyndhurst and Dervla Kirwan

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Poplar for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: A group of young amateur sleuths put their heads together as they attempt to find out what happened to missing horticulturist Wesley Shaw, while Dave Barnes digs deep to plant a collection of exotic palms. Narrated by Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Friend of the Royal Botanic Gardens Margaret Leigh is driven into a bidding frenzy at a plant auction in the grounds. Meanwhile, Steve Robinson makes his way out to Kew's country counterpart, Wakehurst Place, to put a contemporary spin on a centuries-old tradition. Narrated by Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Bloody Mama (1970)
programme.desc: A poverty-stricken psychotic woman goes on a rampage against society along with her homicidal children, robbing banks, kidnapping the wealthy and resorting to murder at the drop of a hat. Thriller based on the story of the infamous Ma Barker, starring Shelley Winters, Robert De Niro, Robert Walden, Don Stroud, Clint Kimbrough. Pat Hingle and Bruce Dern

programme.title: The Bromley Boys (2018)
programme.desc: In 1969, with England preparing for the defence of their World Cup crown, 15-year-old David Roberts falls in love with his local non-league club, Bromley FC. While trying to enter the inner sanctum of the club's most loyal fans, David embarks on a seemingly sham relationship with the chairman's daughter, Ruby. Comedy, starring Brenock O'Connor and Jamie Foreman

programme.title: Relentless (2020)
programme.desc: An ex-convict must stay out of trouble for one more day to secure his freedom - but must choose between his principles and his future when he comes to the aid of a prostitute under threat from a gang. Action thriller, starring Steven Murphy and Tiffany-Ellen Robinson

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: A drunk John Hurt arrives in the neighbourhood, while Joe takes advantage of Michael's absence by taking over his house. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Queensbury Boxing League
programme.desc: Boxers battle it out over three four and five two minute rounds against opponents - the most successful competitors will earn the right to challenge for the coveted Queensbury British title

programme.title: Legend of the Dragon
programme.desc: Animated adventures for children

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures.

programme.title: Kong: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Kong, Jason, jungle girl Lua, fun-loving Tann and wise Dr. Jenkins take on power-hungry scientists, ferocious prehistoric creatures and incredible magical beings to protect their unique island home.

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Sail the Saronic and Ionian Greek islands with Peter Maneas as he explores their rich history, culture, cuisine, and people as well as their hidden secrets and surprises

programme.title: Hatton Garden: The Inside Story
programme.desc: Ross Kemp examines the April 2015 heist in London's diamond district, when a group of career criminals broke into a safe deposit facility and stole approximately £14million in precious metals, cash and jewels, an event dubbed the biggest burglary of its type in British history. The documentary gains access to the secret surveillance material that put the thieves behind bars and features exclusive interviews with the detectives who cracked the case as well as Kenny Collins, one of the thieves involved

programme.title: The Edgar Wallace Mystery Theatre
programme.desc: Two men visit a gambler - each with a reason to kill him

programme.title: Quiet Weekend (1946)
programme.desc: Members of a family are plunged into a series of awkward situations while trying to enjoy a quiet weekend in the country. Comedy, starring Derek Farr and Frank Cellier

programme.title: Dandy Dick (1935)
programme.desc: A church vicar tries a variety of schemes to come up with money to fix the church's steeple. When his plans fail, he bets his savings on a horse race. Comedy, starring Will Hay

programme.title: Death at Broadcasting House (1934)
programme.desc: When an actor is murdered, Detective Inspector Gregory quickly discovers that the victim had many enemies. Drama, starring Jack Hawkins, Donald Wolfit and Ian Hunter

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: A group of young amateur sleuths put their heads together as they attempt to find out what happened to missing horticulturist Wesley Shaw, while Dave Barnes digs deep to plant a collection of exotic palms. Narrated by Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Friend of the Royal Botanic Gardens Margaret Leigh is driven into a bidding frenzy at a plant auction in the grounds. Meanwhile, Steve Robinson makes his way out to Kew's country counterpart, Wakehurst Place, to put a contemporary spin on a centuries-old tradition. Narrated by Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: London Live News
programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital

programme.title: Goodnight Sweetheart
programme.desc: Gary realises that life in 1941 is not what he expected when he is put on fire-watching duty, while Phoebe is distressed to discover her relationship is the subject of gossip. Starring Nicholas Lyndhurst and Dervla Kirwan

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring Thamesmead for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: St Paul's
programme.desc: Part two. The Dean and Chapter are forced to consider increasing admission cost to help maintain the building

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom lands a date with the college administrator, but ends up sharing the evening with Roy, so they conspire to charm her jointly - until their egos get in the way, leading to a battle for her affections. Comedy, starring Jane Asher, Roger Lloyd Pack, Clive Swift and Tessa Wyatt

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael throws a fancy dress party at his house to celebrate the 33rd anniversary of the film Zulu, but the mood turns sour when the Monopoly board comes out

programme.title: Awaydays (2009)
programme.desc: A bored and disaffected young man seeks an outlet for his pent-up aggression. He befriends a member of a local football hooligan group and falls in with the crowd, finding release in drink, drugs, football and fighting. Drama set in the 1980s, based on Kevin Sampson's novel, starring Nicky Bell, Stephen Graham, Liam Boyle and Oliver Lee

programme.title: Red Army Hooligans (2018)
programme.desc: The leader of a hooligan gang sustains a head injury in a fight with a Russian rival, that could bring an end to his life of violence. However, when both factions find themselves banned from the world cup, he challenges the Russians to come to London and face him and his followers in a series of street battles. Crime thriller, starring Jon-Paul Gates and Earl Ling

programme.title: The Starving Games (2013)
programme.desc: A futuristic society challenges teenagers from impoverished communities to fight to the death for food. A girl is persuaded to take her sister's place in the games, but is unprepared for the bizarre selection of characters waiting for her. Spoof of The Hunger Games, starring Maiara Walsh and Brant Daugherty

programme.title: News Rewind
programme.desc: A digest of news and events from across London from the past few days

programme.title: Talking Points
programme.desc: A chance to catch up on debates about the issues behind the headlines

programme.title: Live One-Day International Cricket
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Coverage of the first match in the three-match series, taking place at Sophia Gardens, Cardiff. The visitors haven't beaten England in a one-day series since a 3-2 victory in Pakistan in 2006, a run that stretches back seven series. The last five series between the two sides has seen England win at least three matches in each series, including a 4-0 victory back in 2019.

programme.desc: The ex Manchester-United and England defender talks to former Liverpool, United and Three Lions striker Michael Owen about the some of the matches they have played in both as teammates and rivals. Owen burst onto the scene as a teenager, scoring a famous goal against Argentina in the last 16 of the 1998 World Cup and won the Ballon d'Or in 2001, but also suffered with injuries throughout his career
